6.6.19 Machine Tool Control (T Command)

Tool changes are executed via servo axes controlled by the system. The T-code specifies the tool number in the program.

  • Command Format

T □□△△                    

□□：Tool Number

  • Turret Tool（T01～T09）
  • Lateral Live Tool（T11～T19）
  • Face Tool（T21～T29）
  • Back live tools（T30～T38）

△△：Tool Nose & Wear Offset ID

The4 -digit T code positions the tool:. The first 2 digits indicate the tool number,  and the last 2 digits denote compensation. T0000  cancels compensation. The X、Y、Z coordinates for each tool (tool number) are stored in the system's tool length registry.

Note:

  1. Tool selection defaults to rapid feed motion.
  2. T codes must be in standalone program blocks.
  3. Do not combine T-codes (T□□△△) with M/S-codes in the same block.
  4. Unauthorized personnel must not modify tool length values. Always back up data before changes.

6.7 Machine Setup and Adjustment Instructions

6.7.1 Machine Linear Axes

The machine is equipped with 5 servo-driven linear axes:Y1 and X2 axes: Perform tool-changing motions.X axis: Controls radial positioning and radial cutting feed.Z1 and Z2 axes: Control axial positioning and axial cutting feed. All servo axes utilize absolute encoders for position feedback.

6.7.2 Spindle Rotation Direction

When facing the spindle from the right side of the machine:Counterclockwise (CCW) rotation is defined as spindle forward (M03).Clockwise (CW) rotation is defined as spindle reverse (M04).

6.7.3 Tool Specifications

  1. Turning Tool Holder for External Machining □16×100~140、6 pcs；
  2. Face Drilling Tool Holder Shank：Φ32（Standard）；
  3. Face Drilling Axis: Equipped with ER20 collets.
  4. Side Milling Live Tool Axis: 1× ER20 collets + 3× ER16 collets.
  5. Back Drilling Axis: ER16 collets.

6.7.4 Machine Spring Collet

  1. Spindle Collet: E32 SC/Φd (0.1 mm increments);
  2. Guide Sleeveing Collet: VNC32 BC/Φd (0.1 mm increments);
  3. Back Spindle Collet: E32 SCU/Φd (0.1 mm increments)；

6.8 Shutdown Procedures

6.8.1 Normal Shutdown During Operation

During automatic program execution:Press the Cycle Stop button on the operator panel to activate M01 (Optional Stop).When the program pauses at M01, press the RESET key to halt the machine.

6.8.2 End-of-Day Shutdown

Follow the Normal Shutdown steps above.Press the red System Power Off button on the operator panel. Confirm the display and indicators are off.Turn the main power breaker to OFF.

6.8.3 Emergency Shutdown

Press the E-stop button (on the operator panel or front control box).Press the red System Power Off button.Turn the main power breaker to OFF.

WARNING: Avoid using the E-stop during normal operation. Emergency stops may damage workpieces, tools, or machine components.

6.9 Pre-Machining Preparations

  1. Turn on Air Supply: Ensure the pneumatic system pressure is 0.5 MPa ±10%.
  2. Lubrication: Verify the lubricant level in the lubrication unit exceeds the minimum mark.
  3. Coolant (if required): Ensure the coolant tank level exceeds the minimum mark.
  4. Confirm the factory voltage matches machine requirements. Turn the main power breaker to ON.
  5. Close the machine tool power circuit breaker to the "ON" position.
  6. Press the "System On" button on the upper part of the operation panel to start the CNC system.
  7. Reset (rotate to the right) the emergency stop button on the main operation panel and front operation box.

If coolant is needed, confirm that the cooling system can work normally: press the "Coolant" button to turn on the coolant, then press the "Coolant" button again to turn off the coolant.

6.10 Installation of Main Spindle Collet and Adjustment of Clamping Force

6.10.1 Spindle Collet Installation

Figure 25 Clamping Mechanism Schematic Main/Sub Spindle Clamping Force Adjustment Procedure：

  1. Loosen the spindle locknut ( Back) counterclockwise to release the collet.
  2. Install the taper sleeve
  3. Install the spring
  4. Install the spindle collet
  5. Install and tighten the spindle nut.
  6. Insert the bar stock through the collet, extending beyond the spindle face.
  7. Clamp the spindle collet.
  8. Rotate the locknut at the Back end of the spindle clockwise until the collet just clamps the bar.
  9. Release the spindle collet.
  10. Continue to rotate the locknut at the Back end of the spindle clockwise about 1/4 turn
  11. Clamp the spindle collet and check if the clamping force is appropriate. Fine-tune the Locking Nut at Spindle Rear to ensure that the spindle has the appropriate clamping force.
  12. Tighten the anti-loosening screw on the side of the locking nut at the back end of the spindle.
  13. Confirm the position of the jaws on the sliding sleeve during clamping, and adjust the relative position of the jaws on the sliding sleeve through the connecting rod on the clamping cylinder.
  14. Tighten the connecting rod and the nut on the cylinder.

6.11 Guide Sleeve Collet Installation and Clamping Force Adjustment

  1. Use a special hex wrench to confirm that the locking screw on the left edge of the collet locking nut has been loosened.
  2. Clean the installation hole of the guide sleeve collet and the guide sleeve collet, and add lubricating oil.
  3. Install the guide sleeve collet into the guide sleeve, aligning the slot on the guide sleeve collet with the pin phase inside the guide sleeve.
  4. Load the bar stock into the Guide Sleeveing, extending it beyond the face of the guide sleeve collet Use a special wrench to rotate the guide sleeve collet locking nut. Gradually tighten the guide sleeve collet.
  5. When tightening the guide sleeve collet, do not clamp the bar too tightly; the bar should be able to be pulled flexibly by hand (otherwise, the collet will be burned). There must be no significant gap between the bar stock and the Guide Sleeveing collet; otherwise, the part’s machining quality will deteriorate.
  6. After adjusting the clamping force of the guide sleeve collet appropriately, use a special hex wrench to tighten the locking screw. Check the clamping force of the guide sleeve collet again. If the clamping force is not appropriate, repeat the above steps.

1.Guide Bushing Housing 2.Guide Collet 3.Locking Nut 4.Clamping Screw 5.Special Wrench6.Lever Arm

6.12 Application of Bushingless Mode

This machine tool is capable of operating in bushingless mode.

For parts with a length-to-diameter ratio of less than 2.5, the bushingless mode can be selected to effectively reduce the length of the remaining material.

Maximum part length for bushingless mode: 80mm.

Before using the machine, the bushing mode must be correctly configured. Different bushing modes will invoke different Z1 -axis soft limits.

On the initial page of the NC system, navigate to the right and find the "SYIL Machinery" function option in the last item. Within the "SYIL Machinery" function option, locate the "Bushing" setting option. Use the up and down arrow keys to select the appropriate bushing mode.

Figure 28 Bushing Mode Setting Screen

Figure 29 Rotary Guide Sleeve Transmission Diagram

Figure 30 Bushingless Machining Diagram

Steps to Replace Rotary Bushing with Bushingless Machining:

  1. Loosen the two M6 screws connecting spindle front cover and spline shifting fork
  2. Remove the protective cover at the front end of the spindle.
  3. Disengage the spindle drive belt.
  4. Rotate spline shifting fork rearward and secure bracket to column
  5. Install guide-free spindle protective cover
  6. Remove guide sleeve protective cover
  7. Disengage the rotary guide sleeve drive belt.
  8. Remove the rotary guide sleeve assembly.
  9. Install the protective sleeve for the bushingless device.
  10. Set the machine to bushingless mode in the CNC system. The switch is now complete.

6.13.2 Installation of External Cutting Tools

Figure 31 External Turning Tool Installation 

  1. Select "MDI" mode via the mode selector rotary switch on the operation panel
  2. Use the NC function key to select the "Machining Info" menu and enter the MDI machining info page.
  3. Use the NC function key to select the "MDI Input" menu to bring up the MDI input page.
  4. Press  button on the control panel to illuminate it.
  5. Enter the following program code (example for installing T02 Φ16mm): T0202 //Call T02 tool
    G00 Y0
    G00 X16.0
  6. Extend the material approximately 15mm beyond the bushing end face.
  7. Loosen the tool clamp screw.
  8. Insert the tool into the tool slot, ensuring it is flush with the bottom of the slot.
  9. Gently tighten the tool clamp screw to prevent the tool from falling out.
  10. Use the mode selection switch on the control panel to switch the machine to MPG mode.
  11. Move the carriage in the "X+" direction to separate the tool from the material.
  12. Tighten the tool clamp screw to secure the tool.

6.13.3 Installation of Front Drilling Tools

Figure 32 Front Drilling Tool Installation Schematic1

  1. Assemble the drilling tool to the drilling spindle.
  2. Insert the drilling tool and spindle assembly into the drilling tool holder.
  3. Use a dedicated drilling tool alignment fixture to position the drilling tool assembly.
  4. Tighten the locking screw on the side of the drilling tool holder to secure the drilling tool assembly.

Figure 34 Side Milling Cutter Installation Schematic

  1. Select "MDI" mode via the mode selector rotary switch
  2. Use the NC function key to select the "Machining Info" menu and enter the MDI machining info page.
  3. Use the NC function key to select the "MDI Input" menu to bring up the MDI input page.
  4. Press ，button on the control panel to illuminate it.

    |G00 Y0 G00 X16.0
  6. Install the corresponding side live tool in the T11 tool position (do not tighten yet).
  7. Extend the bar stock beyond the end face of the Guide Sleeveing collet
  8. Pull the side live tool out of the power spindle until it contacts the outer diameter of the bar stock. Use a dedicated wrench to gently tighten the ER collet nut to prevent the tool from falling out.
  9. Use the mode selection switch on the control panel to switch the machine to MPG mode.
  10. Move the carriage in the "X+" direction to separate the tool from the material.
  11. Tighten the corresponding ER collet nut to secure the tool.

6.14 Use of the Feeder

This machine can be equipped with an automatic multi-bar feeder according to processing needs. During long-term mass production, manual material replacement is no longer required, as the bar feeder automatically completes the task.

6.14.1 Bar Feeder-Related M Codes

M60：Activate the bar feeder material change cycle

M61：Spindle jog – ON

M62：Spindle jog – OFF

M63:    Bar feeder thrust-ON

M64:    Bar feeder thrust-OFF

M65:    Bar feeder retract allowed – ON

M66:    Bar feeder retract allowed – OFF

6.14.2 Precautions for Using the Bar Feeder

  1. Adjust the bar feeder thrust appropriately based on the size, material, and weight of the bar stock.
  2. Refer to the relevant bar feeder manual for setting bar feeder parameters.
  3. Ensure the collet at the front of the push rod rotates smoothly.
  4. Select appropriate feed channel assembly and ejector rod accessories corresponding to the diameter of the machined bar stock
  5. If necessary, install a vibration-damping bushing on the spindle based on the size of the bar stock.

6.15 Adjustment of the EjectorDevice

The ejector device is used to discharge workpieces from the back spindle To handle situations where the workpiece cannot be discharged smoothly after the eject command, a safety device with stop alarm isequipped.  When replacing the ejector head, select one that matches the diameter of the workpiece. If the standard ejector head does not meet the processing requirements, the user must fabricate a custom ejector head.

6.15.1 Diagram of Ejector Device

1. Pneumatic Blow Connector 2. Adapter Plate 3. SupportSleeve  4. Clamping Screw 5. Support Tube 6. Ejector Rod 7. Ejector Head 8. Ejector Cylinder

Figure 40: Diagram of Ejector Device

6.15.2 Installation and Adjustment of Ejector Device

  1. Insert the (5) support tube into the (3) support sleeve.
  2. Tighten the (4) locking screw.
  3. Mount the (3) support sleeve and (5) support tube onto the spindle bracket, and tighten the fixing screws of the support sleeve.
  4. Assemble (6) Ejector Rod to (2) Adapter Plate
  5. Attach the air blow connector to the (5) ejector tube.
  6. Install the (7) ejector head onto the (6) ejector tube.
  7. Insert the (6) ejector tube assembly into the (5) support tube, then reciprocate the (6) ejector tube to verify smooth sliding motion
  8. Mount the (2) connection plate onto the (8) ejector cylinde
  9. Operate the（8） ejector cylinder to move the（6）ejector tube. When the (6) ejector tube extends, adjust its position（6） relative to the (2) connection plate to ensure the ejector head protrudes 1–2mm from the back spindle chuck face. Tighten the locking screws on the (2) connection plate to secure the ejector tube.  
  10. Repeatedly extend and retract the ejector cylinder to ensure smooth operation without jamming. Secure the (2) connection plate to the ejector cylinder.

Note: If the (6) ejector tube jams during operation, an ejector malfunction alarm will be triggered. Adjustthe concentricity of the (5) support tube relative to the spindle bore to ensure smooth（6） ejector tube movement.

6.18 Part Machining Procedure

The procedure for formal part machining is shown in Figure below.

Figure 45 Part Machining Flowchart

Special attention should be paid to the following items during the part machining procedure:

  1. If there is an error in the part machining program, it must be corrected before continuing to run the program.
  2. After programming, the program must be run without tools installed to perform a dry run. Only after the dry run is error-free can subsequent work continue.
  3. Install the tools and the part blank. Position each tool to the pre-feed position according to the programming data and confirm if there is any possibility of interference or tool collision.

Press the "Single Block" button on the operation panel, lower the feed rate override knob value, and run the program in single block mode. Only after the previous block runs without errors can the next block be executed. During single block operation, closely monitor for any command errors or tool offset setting errors that may cause discrepancies. If any potential error actions are detected, immediately press the emergency stop button, exit the operation, and resolve all errors before resuming automatic operation.

To predict machine failures or detect them early and minimize production losses, regular inspections of the machine should be conducted regardless of whether there are any faults. This chapter summarizes and explains the necessary inspection items from daily checks to those required every six months.

7.1 Routine Maintenance

Daily inspections are simple checks that should be performed before and during the operation of the machine each day. Please follow the inspection points listed below. Additionally, note that the situations requiring attention are not limited to those listed in this chapter. If the machine exhibits abnormal conditions, or if there are noticeable abnormal sounds, temperatures, damages, or odors during operation, inspections must be conducted immediately. Therefore, it is essential to understand the normal operating state of the machine, which is the most effective daily inspection.

7.2 Common Faults and Solutions

This chapter introduces the handling methods for common faults. The methods provided are for common issues, but in practice, faults beyond these may occur. If the cause cannot be identified or resolved, please contact Manufacturer for repair.

7.2.1 Main/Sub-Spindle Collet Failure to Open/Close and Timeout Alarm

If the spindle chuck fails to open, it may result in short workpieces or failure to eject the bar stock.

Troubleshooting steps：

  1. Verify pneumatic supply integrity
  2. Check if the clamping solenoid valve is functioning properly.
  3. Check if the clamping cylinder is functioning properly.
  4. Check if the clamping sensor switch is functioning properly and if it is loose or damaged.
  5. Check if the clamping cylinder connecting rod is loose.
  6. Check if the chuck jaws' travel is normal.
  7. Disassemble the spindle nut, clean the collet and collet cone , and clean the spindle bore.
  8. Check if the collet cone is scratched or if it is stuck in the spindle bore.
  9. Replace the collet with a new one.

If the issue persists after these checks, contact  Manufacturer  for repair.

7.2.2 Low Coolant Flow

The machine uses cutting oil  for cooling. Insufficient cooling may pose a fire hazard. Address low coolant flow promptly.

Troubleshooting steps:

  1. Inspect coolant line connections
  2. Check if the coolant pump is functioning properly.
  3. Check if the coolant tank has sufficient oil.
  4. Clean the chip collection cavity in the machine base.
  5. Clean the coolant tank and filter screen.

Figure 46 Chip Conveyor and Coolant Tank Diagram

Spindle/Sub-spindle Overheat Alarm

The spindle and sub-spindle of this machine are equipped with built-in motor spindles with temperature sensors. An alarm will trigger if the spindle temperature is too high.

Handling Steps:

  1. Check the I/O input module's I16 (spindle overheat detection) and I23 (sub-spindle overheat detection) inputs. A faulty module may cause false alarms. Contact Manufacturer Customer Service Center if the module is damaged.
  2. Check if the spindle temperature is abnormally high and if there is a burning smell. If so, the spindle stator may be damaged. Contact Manufacturer Customer Service Center.
  3. Check if the spindle speed fluctuates abnormally. If so, the spindle stator or encoder may be damaged. Contact Manufacturer Customer Service Center.
  4. Check if the spindle load fluctuates abnormally during idle operation. If so, the spindle stator may be damaged. Contact Manufacturer Customer Service Center.
  5. Check if the spindle's three-phase resistance is balanced. If not, the spindle stator may be damaged. Contact Manufacturer Customer Service Center.
  6. Check the resistance between the spindle's three-phase coils and the spindle housing. If any phase shows finite resistance, the spindle coils may be shorted to the housing. Contact Manufacturer Customer Service Center.
  7. Check if the spindle power cables are damaged or there is a phase loss.
  8. Check if the spindle drive unit is damaged.

Ejector Malfunction Alarm

The counter spindle integrates an ejector mechanism that automatically unloads sub-100mm workpieces through frontal ejection.

If the part is not ejected, it may interfere with subsequent parts or machine components, causing a crash.

For the configuration and calibration of the ejector device, refer to Section 6.16: Installation and Adjustment of the Ejector System.

During ejector operation, if the machine control system fails to detect the signal from the ejector cylinder proximity sensor, a corresponding alarm will be triggered.

Troubleshooting steps：

  1. Verify pneumatic pressure is within specification
  2. Check if the ejector solenoid valve is functioning properly.
  3. Check if the air hoses are damaged.
  4. Check if the ejector cylinder's throttle valve is adjusted properly.
  5. Check if the ejector cylinder operates smoothly without sticking.
  6. Check if the ejector sensor switch is loose or damaged.
  7. Check if the ejector rod is bent or stuck.
  8. Check if there are any residual workpieces or chips in the sub-spindle and clean them.

7.2.3 Torque Limit Exceeded Alarm

During machining, if a drill bit breaks, a tool wears severely, or a cutting tool is damaged, the torque on the axis may spike, risking a crash. Adjusting the torque limits can help prevent such incidents.

Adjust axis torque output limits based on specific machining requirements. Adjust the torque limit parameters as needed.

7.2.4 Lost Position Command Alarm

Alarm Description

The controller stops outputting commands to an axis for 1 second and checks if the feedback matches the command within the allowed error range. If not, this alarm is triggered.

Potential cause

Potential cause     

  1. Mechanical obstruction in motion system
  2. Servo drive unexpectedly enabled or disabled.
  3. Data loss between the CPU board and Axis Control Card due to CPU/Card failure or poor contact.
  4. Poor contact or broken wires in the command transmission line between the controller and servo drive.
  5. Local electromagnetic interference.

Solution              

  1. Verify the integrity of the mechanical lubrication system and proper guideway lubrication
  2. Manually rotate the lead screw to check for jamming.
  3. Check the motor power and encoder cables for proper connection.
  4. Check for damage in the motor power and encoder cables.
  5. Check if the drive is powered properly and if there is any phase loss.

After the alarm, without shutting down, check if diagnostic data 8, 9, and 10 converge to 0. If not, re-home the axis and observe diagnostic data 24, 25, 26, 40, 41, and 42. If any of 24, 25, or 26 are non-zero, there is a feedback loop issue. If any of 40, 41, or 42 are non-zero, there is a command loss issue. If both sets have non-zero values, signal interference is likely. Specifically, during machining, the values of variables 8, 9, and 10 gradually increase. The possible fault point may be poor contact between the CPU board and the axis control card. Try replacing the CPU board and the axis control card.The motor or drive may be damaged. Try replacing them.

7.2.5 Following Error Alarm, Severe Following Error Alarm

Alarm Description

Due to servo characteristics, the servo motor's positioning may lag behind the controller's command. If this lag exceeds the allowed range, a following error alarm is triggered.

Potential cause   

  1. Irregular mechanical movement
  2. Poor cable contact
  3. Controller acceleration/deceleration time is set too short.
  4. Drive or motor failure.

Troubleshooting steps        

  1. Inspect the lubrication circuit to ensure adequate lubrication at all machine points.
  2. Check the machine's wiring to ensure all connections are secure and undamaged.
  3. Adjust the acceleration/deceleration time for the affected axis.
  4. Run the machine idle to confirm smooth operation without interference.
  5. If the alarm persists, contact Manufacturer Customer Service

Center. Note:

  1. Diagnostic variable 32: X-axis allowable following error.
  2. Diagnostic variable 33: Y-axis allowable following error.

Diagnostic variable 34: Z-axis allowable following error.

7.2.6 Low Absolute Position Encoder Battery

The servo motors of this machine tool are equipped with absolute position encoders. After the machine is powered off and restarted, there is no need to perform a reference point return operation, and machining can be directly carried out. When the machine is powered off, the servo motor encoders record the position information of each linear axis. The power for the servo motor encoders is supplied by a battery located in the electrical cabinet at the rear of the machine. When the battery voltage is low, the system will issue a "Low Absolute Position Encoder Battery" alarm. Please replace the battery promptly.

Precautions for Battery Replacement

  1. Battery Specification: LR20 type (i.e., D-cell battery).
  2. The battery must be replaced while the machine is powered on; otherwise, the absolute position data will be lost.

Figure 48 Battery Compartment Location Diagram

7.2.7 Absolute Position Zero Point Lost

The servo motors of this machine tool are equipped with absolute position encoders. After the machine is powered off and restarted, there is no need to perform a reference point return operation, and machining can be directly carried out. When the machine is powered off, the servo motor encoders record the position information of each linear axis.  If the system fails to read the position data from the servo motor encoders when the machine is powered on, this alarm will be triggered.

Possible Causes

  1. The machine has been idle for an extended period, and the absolute position encoder battery has been depleted.
  2. The absolute position encoder battery was not replaced in time when it was low.
  3. The motor or drive unit is damaged.
  4. A new motor has been installed.

Troubleshooting Steps:

  1. If the machine is not used for an extended period, it should be powered on at least once a week.
  2. Replace the absolute position encoder battery promptly when it is low.
  3. Reset the absolute position zero point.

Machine Zero Point Reference: Refer to Section 5.6 the machine travel diagram.

Z1 Axis Zero Point: 5mm from the positive hard limit block of the Z1 axis.

X1 Axis Zero Point: The center of the T24 drilling tool position coincides with the spindle center. Y1 Axis Zero Point: The right face of the T01 tool coincides with the spindle center.

X2 Axis Zero Point: The sub-spindle center coincides with the spindle center Z2 Axis Zero Point: 5mm from the negative hard limit block of the Z2 axis.

Steps to Set the Zero Point:

  1. Use the rotary switch on the control panel to switch the machine to MPG control mode.
  2. Use the MPG to move each linear axis to the origin position indicated in the machine travel diagram in Section 5.6.
  3. Use the rotary switch on the control panel to switch the machine to reference point mode.
  4. Use the NC function keys to navigate: "Parameter Setting" → "Serial Parameters" → "General Serial Axis" → "Absolute Zero Point" to enter the mechanical zero point setting interface.

Move the cursor to select the corresponding axis number and complete the zero point setting.

7.2 Maintenance Precautions

  1. Qualified maintenance personnel should familiarize themselves with the electrical control principles and operating procedures of the machine before performing any repairs.
  2. Regularly clean the dust inside the electrical enclosure during machine operation.
  3. Ensure the power is turned off before cleaning.If the machine is idle for an extended period, power it on at least once a week and let it run at low speed for at least 2 hours.
  4. When a fault occurs, first check whether the power supply and control voltages are normal, whether the circuit breakers and switches are in the correct state, and whether all connectors and plugs are securely connected and not loose.
  5. Do not plug or unplug any connectors while the machine is powered on.
  6. Do not solder electronic circuits while the soldering iron is powered on.
  7. The positions of switches and limit blocks on the machine are pre-adjusted before leaving the factory. Users should not adjust them casually to avoid affecting normal operation.

Note: If the user cannot resolve the fault, please contact our company promptly for proper handling.

The overall layout of the machine tool is shown in Figure 5.

  1. Base     2. Spindle   3. Back Drilling Tool            4. Column             5. Tool Magazine                
  2. Lateral Live Tool  7. Back Spindle   8. Face Drilling Tool Holder   9. Back Spindle Carriage Assembly

Figure 14 Overall Layout of the Machine

Tool Base: Mainly used to support various components.

Column: The column is equipped with X-axis and Y-axis slides and a tool magazine system. The machine tool performs tool changing through the movement of the Y-axis slide. The machine tool achieves radial feed motion of the tool through the up and down movement of the X-axis.

Spindle: The spindle motor is a built-in motor (commonly referred to as an electric spindle), driven by a frequency converter with stepless speed regulation. The spindle housing contains a spindle speed detection encoder. The machine tool achieves axial feed motion of the tool through the Z-axis ball screw driving the spindle forward and backward.

Pneumatic System: The pneumatic system is located at the lower Back of the machine tool. The machine tool controls the clamping/releasing of the spindle and the extension/retraction of the cylinder through the pneumatic system.

Lubrication System: The lubrication system is located at the lower Back of the machine tool. The machine tool provides lubrication to the ball screws and linear guideways through the lubrication system to ensure long-term stable operation.

Coolant Tank: The coolant tank is located below the right side of the base and can be pulled out to the right for easy cleaning.

The main electrical control cabinet is installed in a recess on the lower left side of the base. The left side of the base has an electrical cabinet protective door with a power switch and ventilation device.

The control panel is located on the swing arm at the front of the machine tool. It can be pulled out during machine tool setup for convenient operation by workers. The CNC panel is equipped with two USB 2.0 ports for data transfer. A handheld pulse generator is also provided on the side of the control panel.

The machine tool is equipped with a fully enclosed protective cover. The right side has a flip door with a tempered glass observation window for monitoring the machine tool's operation during machining. Opening the door provides access to the main cutting area for tool adjustment, tool setting, and coolant flow adjustment. The left side has two sliding doors that provide access to the spindle area for adjusting spindle and guide sleeve clamping force.

5.1 Spindle and Sub-Spindle

The machine tool's spindle and sub-spindle are equipped with an integrated motor spindle structure. To ensure thermal stability, the spindle housing is cooled using a circulating oil cooling system. It is recommended to use Great Wall No. 7 spindle oil (or Mobil No. 4 spindle oil) for spindle cooling.

Since the front end of the spindle is protected by an air curtain seal, a slight hissing sound from the front end of the spindle is normal. Adjust the air pressure of the air curtain seal to between 0.2 and0.4 MPa.

Spindle and Sub-Spindle Diagram

5.2 Feed System

The machine tool has five linear axes: X1, Y1, Z1, X2, and Z2.

The rotational axes include S1 (main spindle), S2 (sub-spindle), S3 (lateral live tool spindle), and S5 (Back live tool spindle). Among these, S1 (main spindle) and S2 (sub-spindle) have C-axis functionality. The machine tool system is a dual-channel control system. The first channel includes X1, Y1, Z1, S1 (main spindle), and S3 (lateral live tool spindle). The second channel includes X2, Z2, S2 (sub-spindle), and S5 (back tool spindle).

 

Machine Tool Tool Layout Diagram

5.3 Cooling System

The coolant tank is installed under the right side of the machine base and can be pulled out to the right for easy cleaning. During machining, select the appropriate cutting oil based on the material of the workpiece being processed.

  • Water-soluble cutting oil is not recommended.
  • Do not use cutting oil with high volatility or low flash points.

 Cooling System Diagram

5.4 Pneumatic System

The machine tool's pneumatic system is installed in the pneumatic box at the Back right of the machine.

Adjust the main air supply pressure to approximately 0.5 MPa, ensuring the main air supply is dry and stable. Adjust the air seal pressure to between 0.08 and 0.1 MPa.

Pneumatic System Diagram


Pneumatic Schematic Diagram

Pneumatic principle

5.5 Lubrication System

The machine tool's ball screws and guideways are lubricated through a centralized lubrication system. The lubrication pump is installed on the back of the machine, in a niche on the base. It is recommended to use ISO VG 32lubricating oil. The system can be configured for lubrication duration and intervals. It is recommended to lubricate every 30 minutes for 5 seconds each time (for new machines, the lubrication duration can be appropriately extended).

Lubrication pump oiling time parameter: 3429 (unit:seconds)

Lubrication pump oiling interval parameter: 3430 (unit: minutes)

The lubrication pump is equipped with an oil level detection switch. The machine will trigger an alarm when the oil level is too low.

Article subtitle: S205/S265 SWISS-TYPE CNC LATHE S206/S266 SWISS-TYPE CNC LATHE (For SYNTEC systems) Operation Manual 2026
Article body:
This manual is only the operating instructions of this machine tool, for instruction on the use of other parts, please refer to the following technical documents provided at random.

  • SYNTEC controller specification
  • SYNTEC programming specification

Before using the machine tool, must fully grasp the contents of this manual and other instructions provided at random, to use the machine tool safely and correctly.

Operators must undergo adequate training in the safe use, adjustment and operation of machine tools before they can use machine tools.

2.1    Personal protection warning message


This machine tool has relevant warning signs on the parts that may cause personal injury, IT can serve as a warning to operators. So taking off the warning signs is never allowed. Operators should pay attention to personal protection and avoid personal injury according to the warning.

Fig.1 Schematic diagram of the location of the warning sign 1

 Fig.2 Schematic diagram of the location of the warning sign 2 

Warning signs 1

Warning signs 2

Warning signs 3

Warning signs 4

Warning signs 5

Warning signs 6

Warning signs 7

Warning signs 8

Warning signs 9

2.2    Safe device warning message

The relevant safely devices cannot guarantee absolute safety in the event of accidents and dangerous situations. Be sure to observe the relevant precautions when using this machine tool.

2.2.1    Installation location of safely device

The installation location of the safely device is shown in Fig.3 .

 

Fig.3 Position diagram of machine safety device

2.2.2    Functional description of the safely device

1）Door switch (opt.)
The machine tool will not be able to operate in automatic mode as long as the door is not fully closed. When the machine is in the automatic mode, the machine tool will switch to the feed hold state if the door is opened. 
In the manual mode, even if the door is not closed, the switches about spindle collet clamping, spindle rotating and cutting oil, can still be operated in order to facilitate parts’ adjusting and cutting.

2）Main circuit breaker

If excessive current is detected in the machine tool , the power supply will be cut off automati-cally. 

3）Emergency stop button 
There is a red emergency stop button on the machine tool operation. When there is an emer-gency such as fire, earthquake or lightning and emergency refuge is necessary, press this but-ton before leaving the machine tool to stop the machine tool.

Pressing the emergency stop button during operation of the machine tool will not only damage the machined parts and tools, but also damage the machine tool. 

To release the emergency stop state, you must confirm that it is safe, and then rotate the emer-gency stop button clockwise.

2.2.3    Precautions before operation

1)    Before starting the machine tool, the operator must master the following
•    Location of the emergency stop button
•    The contents of all warning signs
•    The way to stop the machine tool in an emergency
•    The function, operation method and operation sequence of the switches and buttons on the operation panel
•    The correct order of start and end 
•    The method of handing when an abnormal situation occurs
•    Cutting off method of main Circuit Breaker of Machine tool 
2)    When the operator is harmful to personal safely and the safe operation of the machine tool , such as drinking , excessive fatigue and poor health , please do not operate the machine tool .
3)    Operators should wear appropriate overalls , hats and safety shoes , gloves , rings , ties , necklaces and other dangerous items that may be involved in the machine tool . please remove them in advance .  
4)    To prevent slipping , falling or tripping around the machine tool , keep the machine tool clean and tidy .
5)    Fire-prone objects should not be placed around the machine tool . 
6)    Please do not run the machine tool when the safety device does not work .
7)    Please make sure that the emergency stop button is function properly .
8)    Do not modify the machine tool and control circuit privately , otherwise it may cause personal injury or damage to the machine tool .
9)    Please use a lubricating , non-corrosive and non-flammable cutting fluid .
10)    Please make sure that the tools and fixtures are installed correctly before the machine tool is running .
11)    Before running the machine tool , please make sure that the amount of coolant is suffi-cient and the cutting part is fully cooled . Regular removal of chips in cutting parts and chip collection devices .
12)    It is forbidden to remove the shield of the machine tool to run the machine tool !
13)    It is prohibited to process flammable , explosive and dusty materials !

2.2.4    Matters needing attention during operation

1)    Please do not open the electric box protection door when the main circuit breaker is in “ON” .
2)    During the operation of  the machine tool , please do not touch or approach the cutting tool and the moving parts of the machine tool .
3)    Please confirm carefully before pressing the relevant operation button.

2.2.5    Matters needing attention for shutting down machine tools

Order in which machine tools are turned off :
1.    Reset and stop machine tool operation
2.    Press the emergency stop button
3.    Cut off  the power of  NC
4.    Cut off  the main circuit breaker

2.2.6    Measures to deal with emergencies 

1)    Measures to be taken in case of emergency such as fire, earthquake or thunder
•    Have plenty of  time
1.    Stop the machine tool from running 
2.    Cut off  the power of  NC
3.    Cut off  the main circuit breaker
•    Time is tight
1.    Press the emergency stop button
2.    Cut off the main circuit breaker

2)    Sudden power outage 
•    Treatment after sudden power outage:
1.    Press the emergency stop button
2.    Cut off the main circuit breaker

3)    Steps to resume machine tool operation after an emergency
1.    Power on the machine tool and restart the machine tool
2.    Check the damage of the tool
3.    Replace the broken toll
4.    Cut off or take out parts that were not finished during a power outage 

2.2.7    Matters needing attention in storage and transportation of machine tools 

1)    The machine tool is packed with rainproof, and coated with antirust oil on the key parts. The packaging box adopts certain anti-vibration and anti-shock measures to ensure safe transportation and storage in the temperature range of -25℃~55℃. However, the pack-aging box must not be allowed to be turned upside down or tilted through 15℃, and no violent impact or vibration is allowed to avoid damage to the internal devices.
2)    When the machine tool is not in operation for a long time, it is necessary to protect the machine tool with anti-rust oil and remove the power inlet; cover the machine with a film sleeve to prevent dust; block the power inlet hole in the electrical niche of the machine base to prevent rodent damage.

5.1    Machine Removal

After opening the outer packaging box of the machine tool, firstly remove the random tools, cooling boxes and other accessories on the bottom plate of the packaging box, and then re-move the four fastening nuts at the connection between the machine tool and the bottom plate.

5.2    Machine hoisting, positioning

5.2.1    Machine hoisting

1)    Two Φ45mm diameter steel rods (made of high-quality low-carbon steel No. 20, one is 2 m long, the other is 1.5 m long, with a safety factor greater than 4) penetrate into the holes of 2 sets of M20 rings, as shown in Fig.4 The hook is used to hook the hoisting ca-ble on the steel rod (the safety factor of the hoisting cable is greater than 5, the hoisting cable should not touch the parts of the machine tool, and if necessary, use a wooden block to firmly and reliably open the hoisting cable and the machine tool parts Or use a special hanger).

Fig.4 Machine hoisting

2)    Raise the lifting cable gradually, check the lifting cable and the center of gravity of the machine tool to ensure safety

3)    When the machine is transported to the installation site and the machine is still in the air, screw the six leveling screws and lock nuts provided together, and screw the level-ing screws from the foot installation cavity into the six foot screw holes on the base In-side, make the ball protrude from the bottom surface of the base.

4)

Fig.5  Installation of machine tool foot screws

5)    Place the six supplied foot pads correctly in the machine tool placement position as shown in Fig.6.

Fig.6 Machine installation

6)    Let the machine tool down lower gradually and adjust the position to align the six blocks with six leveling screws. so that the six leveling screws are aligned with the six pads.
7)    Place the machine tool steadily on the six foot pads and adjust the six leveling screws so that the distance between the bottom surface of the machine tool base foot screw and the ground is 35mm.

Please observe the following precautions when lifting the machine, otherwise it may cause the machine to fall (damage) and cause serious personal injury.
1)    Hoisting personnel must have nationally recognized qualification certificates
2)    Prohibited rolling
3)    The non-slip mat (rubber or cloth) must be added to the part where the lifting rod and the lifting cable contact.
4)    Ensure that the lifting cable can withstand the weight of the machine tool (bout 3500kg) 
5)    During the hoisting process, the machine tool should not be inclined, and try to avoid impact and vibration.

5.2.2    Machine lifting

Fig.7 Forklift

Please observe the following precautions when lifting the machine, otherwise it may cause the machine to fall (damage) and cause serious personal injury.
1)    Lifting personnel must have nationally recognized qualification certificates
2)    Forklift tonnage requirement:  ≥5T
3)    During the lifting process, the machine tool should not be inclined, and try to avoid impact and vibration.

5.3    Machine installation

5.3.1    Machine foundation requirements

1)    The load bearing capacity of the foundation of the installed machine tool is not less than 3000kg/m2, and the thickness of the foundation is not less than 150mm.
2)    The foundation must extend more than 500mm from the periphery of the machine tool.
3)    The machine tool should be installed on a solid, level ground, and should not be af-fected by the vibration of other machines.
4)    When the machine tool is installed on the floor, the structural strength and vibra-tion frequency of the floor should be fully evaluated, and it should be approved by the plant design unit or the authority of the authority.
5)    The installation position of the machine tool should have enough operation and maintenance space.

5.3.2    Usage environment of machine tool

1)    Ambient temperature: 0℃～45℃ during operation; -20℃～55℃ during non-operation; temperature change≤20℃/hour.
2)    Relative humidity: usually 10% to 75% (without condensation), short-term (within a month) 5% to 95% (without condensation).
3)    ≤0.5G during operation and ≤1G during non-operation.
4)    The place where chips, water and oil cannot splash.
5)    Stay away from electric welding and other discharge machining machinery
6)    Keep away from strong electromagnetic interference
7)    Avoid direct sunlight

5.3.3    Machine cooling connection

1)    Push the cooling box under the machine tool from the right side of the machine tool to align it with the sheet metal outside the machine tool.
2)    Put the Chip collection box into the Cooling box and align it with the outlet of the machine tool.
3)    Cover the chip collection box cover.
4)    Connect the cooling pump to the machine tool with hose. Tighten the joint with a throat hoop.
5)    Power on the cooling pump
6)    Plug in the liquid level switch 
7)    Start-up cooling pump, observe the rotation direction of the cooling pump, if the ro-tation direction of the cooling pump is opposite to the direction marked on the pump body, the power phase sequence of the cooling pump needs to be changed, in order to adjust the rotation direction of the cooling pump.
8)    Add matching coolant according to the processed material. Cooling tank volume about 200L.

 

Fig.8 Cooling diagram

5.3.4    Machine tool lubricants

The volume of the lubricating pump oil tank is 2L, and the effective volume is 1.5L. The grade of lubricating oil is: ISO VG-68. It is necessary to add 1.5L of lubricating oil to the lubricating pump for the first time, and the machine tool needs to be replenished in time after a period of operation.

The lubricating oil must be clean, otherwise it will damage the machine guideway and screw.

5.3.5    Machine tool air source requirements

1)    Machine tool air source connected to air pipe：Φ8mm
2)    Machine tool air source requirements: air pressure 0.5MPa, air volume 80Nl/min
3)    Please keep the air source dry and clean. Drain the residual water in the air compressor in time, and add a first-level filtering device to the air compressor if necessary.

5.3.6    Machine tool power supply requirements

1)    power supply & voltage: 3phase 380V±10% 50Hz±2%
2)    Power: 15kVA 

The machine tool ground must be firm and reliable, the neutral line of power supply cannot be used as a protective ground wire！

 

Fig.9 Schematic diagram of external interface of machine tool

5.3.7    Spindle cooling oil

Recommended grade of spindle cooling oil: Mobil 4
The machine tool spindle is cooled by liquid internal circulation, when the machine tool leaves the factory, an appropriate amount of spindle cooling oil has been added. The phenomenon of spindle cooling oil loss may occur in the process of machine tool operation. When using the machine tool for the first time, it is necessary to ensure sufficient cooling oil for the spindle.
There is no loss of cooling oil of the spindle during the normal use of the machine tool. If there is abnormal loss of cooling oil in the spindle, please check whether the cooling oil circuit is dam-aged, necessary times manufacturer maintenance.

Please refer to the relevant operating instructions for the use and maintenance of the oil cooling machine.
Please clean the external filter of the oil cooling machine.

Fig.10 Schematic diagram of filter cleaning

5.3.8    Machine tool adjustment level

1)    Move the spindle box to the position of the Z1 axis negative limit.
2)    Cross the horizontal adjustment plat over the two rails of the Z1 axis.
3)    Put the bar horizontal ruler on the horizontal adjustment board.
4)    Adjust the machine too anchor screw, make the reading of the horizontal ruler with-in 0.04mm/m.
5)    Tighten the locking nut of the anchor screw.

Fig.11  Mounting location for leveling instrument

1.Base   2. Spindle   3. Back drilling tool   4. Column   5. gang tool   6. Side live tool   7. End surface drilling tool  8.Sub spindle   9. drag board assembly

Fig.12 General layout diagram of machine tool

Base: Mainly used to support various components.

Column: X-axis pallet, Y-axis pallet and gang tool system are installed on the upright. The ma-chine tool realizes the tool changing action through the movement of the Y-axis pallet. The ma-chine tool realizes the radial feed motion of the tool through the up and down movement of the X axis.

Spindle: The spindle motor is a built-in motor (electric spindle), which is driven by frequency conversion and stepless speed regulation. The spindle box is equipped with a spindle speed detection encoder. The machine tool drives the spindle forward and backward through the Z-axis screw to realize the axial feed movement of the tool.

Pneumatic system: The pneumatic system is located under the back of the machine   tool. The machine tool controls the clamping/relaxing action of the spindle and the telescopic action of the cylinder through the pneumatic system.

Lubrication system: The lubrication system is located under the back of the machine tool. The machine tool provides lubrication to the ball screw and linear guideway through the lubrication system to ensure the long-term stable operation of the machine tool.
The coolant tank is under the base on the right side of the machine, and can be pulled out to the right to facilitate the cleaning of the coolant tank.

The main control electrical part of the machine tool is installed in the alcove at the lower left of the base. The left side of the base is provided with an electric box protection door. The door is provided with a power switch and an electric box ventilation device.

The control panel is located on the rocker arm on the front of the machine tool, and can be pulled away when the machine tool is debugged, which is convenient for the workers to oper-ate. The NC panel is equipped with two USB2.0 sockets, which can be used for data transmis-sion of the machine tool. A hand-held manual pulse generator is also configured on the side of the control panel.

The machine tool protection adopts a fully enclosed protective cover, a flip-up door is provided on the right side, and a tempered glass observation window is provided on the door, which is convenient for observing the operation of the machine tool during processing. After the door is opened, it is the main cutting area of the machine tool. Tool setting and coolant flow adjustment operations; there is a flip door on the left side. After the door is opened, it is the spindle area of the machine tool, which can modulate the clamping force of the spindle and guide sleeve.

6.1    Main spindle & Sub-spindle

The main spindle and sub-spindle of the machine tool are built-in electric spindle structures. In order to ensure the thermal stability of the main spindle, the main spindle box is cooled by means of circulating oil cooling. It is recommended to use Great Wall 7# spindle oil (or Mobil 4# spindle oil) as the spindle cooling oil.
Because the front end of the main spindle is protected by an air curtain seal, it is normal for the air flow sound at the front end of the main spindle. Adjust the gas pressure of the air curtain seal between 0.2~0.4 MPa.

Fig.13 Schematic diagram of spindle

6.2    Feed system

The linear axis of this machine tool has five linear axes: X1, Y1, Z1, X2, Z2

The rotation axes of this machine tool include S1 (spindle), S2 (sub-spindle), S3 (side live tool axis), and S5 (back live tool axis). And S1 (spindle) and S2 (sub-spindle) have C-axis function.

This machine tool system is a dual-channel control system. X1, Y1, Z1, S1 (spindle), S3 (side live tool axis) belong to the first channel. X2, Z2, S2 (sub-spindle), and S5 (*back driven tool axis) be-long to the second channel.

 

Fig.14  Diagram of the distribution of machine tool S205/S265

 

 

Fig.15  Diagram of the distribution of machine tool S206/S266

6.3    Coolant system

The cooling box is installed under the right base of the machine tool and can be pulled out to the right to facilitate the cleaning of the cooling box. When machining, it is necessary to select the appropriate cutting oil according to the material of the workpiece to be machined.
•     Water-soluble cutting fluids are not recommended.
•     Do not use cutting oil with high volatility and low ignition point.

Fig.16 Diagram of cooling system

6.4    Pneumatic system

The pneumatic system is installed in the pneumatic box at the right rear of the machine tool.

The main air source processing element adjusts the pressure to 0.5 MPa, the main air source should be dry and stable.

The air-tight air source processing element adjusts the pressure to between 0.08~0.1 MPa.

Fig.17 Diagram of the pneumatic system of the machine tool

6.4.1    Pneumatic schematic diagram

Fig.18  Diagram of Pneumatic schematic

6.5    Lubrication system

The lead screw and guide rail of each axis of the machine tool are lubricated by a centralized lubrication system. The machine tool lubrication pump is installed on the back of the machine tool and on the base niche. Lubricant ISO VG 68 guide oil is recommended. The system can set the oiling time and oiling interval of lubricating oil. The lubricating oil setting recommends oiling once every 30 minutes for 5 seconds each time (new machine tools can appropriately extend the oiling time).

Lubricating oil pump filling time parameter:  3429 (setting unit: seconds)

Lubricating oil pump oil filling interval parameter: 3430 (setting unit: minutes) There is a lubricat-ing oil quantity detection switch on the lubricating oil pump. When the lubricating oil quantity is too low, the machine tool will alarm.

Press this button, the LED indicator below the button lights up, and the machine enters the program stop mode.

 

When the machine tool is in automatic operation in this mode, when the "M01" code in the program is executed, the machine tool will change from the "automatic" mode to the "feed hold" mode. Press the "Cycle Start" button on the operation panel again, and the machine tool will continue to execute the program after the "M01" code.

12

Program skip

 

This button is always on.

 

All program segments with “/” will be skipped during execution.

 

The material shortage signal of the bar feeder will turn off the program skip function, and the program with “/” will be executed by the system.

13

Program cycle

 

Press the "program cycle" button, the LED indicator below the button lights up, and the machine enters the "program cycle" mode.

 

In this mode, the machine tool program will automatically cycle when it execute the "M99" code. When the "program cycle" mode is closed, the machine tool program will stop running when it execute the "M99" code.

M04 S□□□□□       spindle ccw

M50                   spindle position mode

7.6.3    Spindle collet control（M10、M11）

Clamp/Loosen the collet assembled in the spindle

The clamp/loosen of the spindle chuck of this machine tool adopts pneumatic control.

There is a spindle clamp/loosen button on the machine operator panel for manual control. When the machine tool is in automatic operation mode, the manual key is invalid, and the cor-responding M code controls the clamping and loosening of the spindle.

  • instruction format

M10         Spindle Collet clamp              

M11         Spindle Collet clamp

7.6.4    Sub-spindle collet control（M20、M21）

Clamp/loosen the collet assembled in the sub-spindle

The clamp/loosen of the sub-spindle chuck of this machine tool adopts pneumatic control.

There is a sub-spindle clamp/loosen button on the machine operator panel for manual control. When the machine tool is in automatic operation mode, the manual key is invalid, and the cor-responding M code controls the clamping and loosening action of the sub-spindle.

•    instruction format
M20         sub-spindle collet clamp                
M21         Sub-spindle collet release

7.6.5    Coolant control（M08、M09）

The cooling system of this machine tool is driven by a three-phase motor. When the machine tool is in manual mode, the button on the operation panel controls the opening or closing of the coolant; when the machine tool is in automatic working mode, it is controlled by M code.

•    instruction format
M08        coolant open
M09        coolant close

7.6.6    High pressure cooling control 

This machine tool can be equipped with a high-pressure cooling device, which can effectively remove the chips wrapped around the tool by configuring the high-pressure cooling device. The high-pressure cooling device draws cutting oil from the cooling oil tank. When using a high-pressure cooling device, the liquid level in the cooling tank will be lower than usual. At this time, if the cooling oil tank is filled with oil, when the cooling stops, it may cause too much oil in the cooling oil tank, causing the cooling oil to overflow the cooling oil tank. When using a high-pressure cooling device, check the amount of oil in the cooling oil tank and maintain an appro-priate amount of oil. The high-pressure cooling device has 4 sets of cooling oil circuits. When more than 2 oil circuits are opened at the same time, the oil pressure sprayed from the nozzle will be lost.


•     instruction format
M104        High pressure 1 on
M105        High pressure 1 Off
M106        High pressure 2 on
M107        High pressure 2 Off
M108        High pressure 3 on
M109        High pressure 3 Off
M110        High pressure 4 on
M111        High pressure 4 Off

7.6.7    Ejection control (M17, M18)

The extension and retraction of the ejector rod of this machine tool are controlled by the air valve.
When the machine tool is in the manual state, the extension and retraction of the ejector rod can be controlled through the material receiving button on the operation panel; when the ma-chine tool is in the automatic processing state, the corresponding M code is executed to control the extension and retraction of the ejector rod.

•    instruction format
M17      ejector rod extended
M18      ejector rod back

1.    When the sub-spindle chuck is in the clamped state or the sub-spindle is running, if the ejector rod extension (M17) is executed, an alarm will be issued.
2.    When the ejector rod is in the extended position, the sub-spindle clamping action (M20) shall not be performed, otherwise an alarm will be issued.
3.    The ejector cylinder is equipped with a detection switch for reaching out position. If the ejector rod extension (M17) action is executed, the detection switch will not detect the relevant signal, and an alarm will be issued.

7.6.8    Parts catcher control（M26、M27）

This machine tool is equipped with a parts conveyor device, which can send the parts ejected from the front end of the sub-spindle to the receiving box outside the machine tool

Outer diameter of transmission parts：≤Φ26mm

Transmission part length：≤80mm

Belt width：  78mm

Command format
M26      Parts Conveyor On
M27      Parts Conveyor Off

1.    Depending on the shape of the parts, there are cases where the parts cannot be dis-charged.
2.    Execute the M26 command, the parts conveyor will automatically stop after rotating for about 15 seconds. There is no need to stop the conveyor with the M27 command.
3.    The running time of the parts conveyor can be adjusted by modifying the value of pa-rameter 3432.

7.6.9    Sub-spindle are blow control (M28, M29)

Chips remaining on the sub-spindle chuck can be cleaned by the sub-spindle blowing function.

•     Command format
M28      Sub-spindle blowing on
M29      Sub-spindle blow off

1.    There is a gas throttle valve on the air blowing line of the sub-spindle, which will affect the air volume and air pressure to a certain extent, and affect the cleaning of the resid-ual chips on the sub-spindle chuck.
2.    Do not cancel the air blowing throttle valve directly, otherwise the system pressure will drop rapidly when the sub-spindle air blowing is opened, which will affect the normal use of the machine tool. If it is necessary to increase the air blowing volume, cancel the gas throttle valve, and need to connect the air source separately from the outside for air blowing of the sub-spindle.
3.    Performing the ejector extension (M17) and retraction (M18) actions at the same time as the sub-spindle blowing can improve the cleaning effect of the remaining chips on the sub-spindle chuck.
4.    The sub-spindle rotates at a low speed while blowing air on the sub-spindle, which can improve the cleaning effect of the remaining chips on the chuck of the sub-spindle.

7.6.10    Side Live Tool Control (M33, M34, M35)

Specifies the side live tool rotation (cw or ccw) and stop rotation.
This machine tool is equipped with 5 side live tools, which can complete the processing of side milling, side drilling, side tapping and other processes. Among them, T11 and T12 tools can be installed with ER11 drill chuck, and T13 and T15 tools can be installed with ER16 drill chuck.

•     Command format
M33 S□□□□□    side live tool rotates forward
M34 S□□□□□    side live tool rotates forward
M35                 side live tool stop

1.    When machining with a side live tool, the feed per minute (G98) is usually used to specify the feed amount of the tool.
2.    If you need to use the feed per revolution (G99) to specify the feed amount of the live tool or use the tapping function of the side live tool, you need to execute the M83 code and specify the live tool axis as the work spindle.

7.6.11    Back Live Tool Control (Optional) (M93, M94, M95)

Specifies the rotation (forward or reverse) and stall of the back force. 

The machine can be equipped with 3 back live tools. By configuring the back side live tool, it is possible to complete the machining of back eccentric holes, back milling grooves, milling and other processes. 3 back live tools are installed on the T32-T34 tool positions to replace the back drilling tools configured on the standard machine. The drill chuck of the ER16 is mounted on the back live tool.

•     Command format
M93 S□□□□□    The back side live tool rotates forward
M94 S□□□□□    The back side live tool rotates reverse
M95                  Backside live tool stall

1. When machining with a side live tool, the feed per minute (G98) is usually used to speci-fy the feed amount of the tool.
2. If you need to use the feed per revolution (G99) to specify the feed of the live tool or use the tapping function of the side live tool, you need to execute the (G88) code and specify the live tool axis as the work spindle.

7.6.12    Specify the work spindle (M81, M82, M83, M85)

The machine tool system defaults that the first channel spindle is the work spindle, and the second channel sub-spindle is the work spindle. When it is necessary to perform linear interpo-lation with other spindles, such as forward-side tapping, back-side tapping, etc., it is necessary to execute the corresponding M code to specify the corresponding spindle as the working spin-dle.

After specifying another spindle as the working spindle, you need to execute the corresponding M code to switch the working spindle back to the main or sub-spindle.

•     Command format
M81      specifies the spindle as the work spindle   
M82      designates the sub-spindle as the work-spindle
M83      designates the side live tool axis as the work spindle
M85      designates the back live tool axis as the work spindle 

7.6.13    Automatic cut off (G266 A* X* S* F* )

In the process of machining parts, it is necessary to stop the operation of the machine tool when encountering abnormal conditions (such as: end of tool life, tool breakage, machine tool abnormality, etc.). After eliminating the abnormal situation of the machine tool, it is necessary to perform cutting head processing before starting the machine tool processing again. The au-tomatic cutting head can be processed by executing the G266 command.

  • Command format

    G266  A* X* S* F*  

    A:Quick positioning position - bar diameter value mm

    X:cutting feed position - cutting diameter mm

    S:Parts cutting speed

    F:Feed rate per revolution

    The G266 command is automatically cut off through the pre-packaged macro program (G266). The macro program content is as follows:

    $1

    %  @MACRO                      // G266 A* X* S* F*  

    WAIT();               

    IF #1 =#0 THEN

    ALARM(100,"G266 A* DATA ERROR");   

    END_IF;

    IF #9 =#0 THEN

    ALARM(101,"G266 F* DATA ERROR");  

    END_IF;

    IF #19 =#0 THEN

    ALARM(102,"G266 S* DATA ERROR");   

    END_IF;

    IF #24 =#0 THEN

    ALARM(103,"G266 X* DATA ERROR");   

    END_IF;

    WAIT(); 

    N000;

    IF(#1036=1) THEN              

    GOTO100;

    ELSE;

    T0101;                                

    END_IF; 

    N100;

    IF (@10721<=#24*1000) THEN     

    IF (@10722=0.0) THEN         

    GOTO 999;

    END_IF;

    END_IF;

    N800;

    M10;

    M08;

    G28 U0.0;                      

    G00 Y0.0;                      

    M03 S#19;                      

    G00 X(#1+2.0);                

    G99 G01 X#24 F#9;             

    M05;

    M09;

    N999;

    M99;

    $2

    M99;

1)    The default T01 tool is the cutting tool. When the T01 tool is not used for cutting, do not execute the command, otherwise there will be a risk of collision.
2)    Before executing the command, the tool No. T01 should be moved to a relatively safe po-sition above the part.

7.6.14    Dual-axis synchronization function ( G200, G113)

Make the speed and phase of the main and sub-spindles consistent. In this state, the machine tool can complete the processing in the following situations:

1.    The workpiece clamped by the main spindle is converted to the action of the sub-spindle.
2.    The main spindle and the sub-spindle clamp a part for processing at the same time.
3.    The phases of the main spindle and the sub-spindle are consistent, and the processing of special-shaped parts is carried out.

•    Command format
G200  S□□□□     The main spindle and the sub-spindle rotate
synchronously at the speed of S□□□□
G113         cancel the synchronization between the main spindle and sub-Spindle

7.6.15    Double Z-axis linkage function (M1000, M1001)

The Z2 axis is linkage with the Z1 axis, and the Z2 axis follows the Z1 axis for synchronous opera-tion. In this state, the machine tool can complete the processing in the following situations:

1.    The main spindle and the sub-spindle clamp a part at the same time for external turn-ing.
2.    The main spindle and the sub-spindle clamp a part at the same time for side milling.
3.    The main spindle and the sub-spindle clamp a part at the same time for turning the ex-ternal thread.

  • Command format

M1000     Start the linkage function of Z1 axis and Z2 axis

M1001     Cancel the linkage function of Z1 axis and Z2 axis

7.6.16    System waiting function (G04.1)

•    Command format

G04.1 P□□   
P□□is the waiting signal code.

The numbers following the P must be encoded in ascending order.
The number of system waiting codes (G04.1 P□□) references must be the same between axis groups. 
When machining with a single axis group, the system waiting code (G04.1 P□□) is invalid.

•     Programming example

7.6.17    Sub-spindle workpiece receiving failure detection (G201)

This command is executed when the cutting tool is detected for cutting. The sub-spindle picks up the workpiece, and after the cutting is completed, cancel the dual-spindle synchronization function, execute the G201 command, and judge whether the cutting tool is broken by detecting the rotation of the sub-spindle.


•     Command format
  G201      Detection of poor work piece access on the back side     

•     Programming example

When the material on the front side and the workpiece clamped on the back side are being sep-arated, for some reasons, although the cutting tool has been broken, the G201 command can-not effectively detect the breakage of the cutting tool. For example, when the cutting tool is damaged, only a small amount is left uncut. After executing the G201 command, the part will be twisted and broken, causing the system to mistakenly believe that the cutting tool is not dam-aged.

7.6.18    Axis torque limit (M74, M75,M76, M77,M78, M79)

The torque of Z1 axis and Z2 axis is limited. When the load of Z1 axis or Z2 axis exceeds the limit value, the machine tool will alarm to achieve the purpose of protecting the machine tool.

•     Command format
M74 D□□    Enable X1/X2 axis torque limit (□□%) 
M75                Cancel X1/X2 axis torque limit
M76 D□□    Enable Y1/Y2 axis torque limit (□□%) 
M77                Cancel Y1/Y2 axis torque limit
M78 D□□    Enable Z1/Z2 axis torque limit (□□%) 
M79                Cancel Z1/Z2 axis torque limit

•     Programming example

1.    This function can be turned on when the main and sub-spindles are drilling. When the drill bit is damaged, the load of the Z1 Z2 motor will increase instantly, triggering an alarm, reminding the customer to replace the drill bit in time to avoid subse-quent tool damage.
2.    This function can be turned on before the synchronous docking of the sub-spindle. When the workpiece on the spindle side has serious chip wrapping or there are re-sidual parts in the chuck of the sub-spindle, the sub-spindle cannot complete the normal docking task, and the Z1/Z2 axis The load of the motor will increase instant-ly, triggering an alarm, reminding the customer to check and improve the working condition of the synchronous docking of the sub-spindle in time.
3.    This function can be turned on before the synchronous docking of the sub-spindle (special-shaped material). When the synchronous phase of the sub-spindle and the main-spindle deviates, the sub-spindle cannot complete the normal docking, and the load of the Z1/Z2 axis motor will increase instantly, triggering an alarm, Remind the customer to correct the synchronization phase deviation between the sub-spindle and the main spindle.
4.    This function can be turned on after the main spindle/sub-spindle synchronous cut-ting is completed. When the cutting tool is damaged, the workpiece is not cut nor-mally, and the sub-spindle retreats, the load of the Z1/Z2 axis motor will increase instantaneously, triggering an alarm, reminding the customer in time Replace bro-ken cutting tools.

7.6.19    Machine tool control (T command)

The tool change action of this machine tool is completed by the system control servo axis. The user uses the method of T code tool change command to designate the required tool number in the preparation of the machining program.

•     Command format
T  □□△△
□□：Tool number

  •     Arrangement of cutters （T01～T09）

  •     Side powered tool（T11～T19）

  •     End face cutter （T21～T29）

  •     Back cutter （T31～T39） 

△△：Tool nose and wear compensation number

Tool positioning is performed with a T-coded 4-digit command. The first 2 digits correspond to the tool number, and the last 2 digits are the compensation number. If T0000 is executed, it rep-resents the compensation cancellation state. The X, Y, Z coordinate values corresponding to □□ (tool number) are recorded in the system tool length value.

1.    The usual tool selections are all run at rapid feed.
2.    Please specify T code in a separate block.
3.    The T code (T □□△△) cannot be the same block as the M code and S code commands.
4.    Non-professionals are not allowed to change the tool length value in the system, and a backup must be made before changing to avoid data loss.

7.7    Machine setting and adjustment

7.7.1    Machine tool coordinate axis setting

This machine tool has 5 servo linear axes: Y1, X2 axis movement realizes tool change action, X axis movement realizes tool radial positioning and radial cut feeding action, Z1, Z2 axis move-ment realizes axial positioning and axial cut feeding function. Each servo axis motor adopts ab-solute encoder.

7.7.2    Spindle rotation direction

Viewed from the right side of the machine tool, the spindle counterclockwise rotation is defined as the spindle forward rotation; the opposite is the reverse rotation.

7.7.3    Tools size

1.    Out diameter cutter ：6×□12-100+1×□16-100；
2.    Dimensions of end face drilling tools：Φ22mm ；
3.    The end face drilling tools used ER16 chuck;
4.    Side power tools use ER11 and ER16 chucks;
5.    Back tool specification: Φ22mm
6.    Back drilling tools used ER16 chuck;

7.7.4    Machine tool chuck

For S205、S206 machine tool: 
1)    Main spindle chuck：S20-SC/Φd（specification0.1 class）；
2)    Guide bushing chuck：S20-BC/Φd（specification0.1 class）；
3)     Sub spindle chuck：S20-SCU/Φd（specification0.1 class）；
4)    For S265、S266 machine tool ：
5)    Main spindle chuck：BU26-SC/Φd（specification0.1 class）；
6)    Guide bushing chuck：BU26-BC/Φd（specification0.1 class）；
7)     Sub spindle chuck：BU26-SCU/Φd（specification0.1 class）；

7.8    The method of machine shutdown

1)    Machine tool shutdown during normal processing
2)    When part processing is in the process of automatic program running, should press "program stop" on the operation panel to make M01 effective (M01 is programmed in the program), when the program stops at M01, press the button "RESET" , the machine tool stop running.
3)    Machine tool shutdown after off work
1.     according to the first method to let the machine tool in shutdown state
2.    press the red "OFF" button on the operation panel, confirm that the display showing is end, and the indicator light on the operation panel is off
3.     press the main circuit breaker, let it at the position “OFF”.
4)    Emergency shutdown
1.    Press the emergency stop button on the operation panel
2.     press the red “OFF” button on the operation panel
3.     press the main circuit breaker, let it at the position “OFF”.

Do not press the emergency stop button if there is no emergency during the machining process. Because press the emergency stop button, all machine action will be forcibly interrupted, will not only damage the processed parts, but also damage the tool and even the machine tool.

7.9    Preparations before processing

1)    Turn on the air source ，confirm that the air pressure gauge reading of the machine tool air system is 0.5MPa±10%.
2)    Confirm that the lubricating oil in the lubrication station exceeds the min. required scale.
3)    If coolant liquid is necessary, confirm that the coolant liquid in the cooling box exceeds the minimum required scale.
4)    Check with the factory power supply voltage, confirm that the power supply meets the requirements, press the factory power circuit breaker.
5)    Press the factory power circuit breaker, let it at the position “ON”.
6)    Press the button "ON" on the system operation panel, CNC system start.
7)    Reset (turn right) the emergency stop button on the operation panel
8)    If need to use coolant liquid, please confirm whether the cooling system can work nor-mally or not: press the "Coolant" button to turn on the coolant, and press the "Coolant" button again to turn off the coolant.

7.10    Installation and Clamping force adjustment of spindle and sub spindle chuck

7.10.1    Installation of spindle chuck

Fig.31  Schematic diagram of clamping mechanism

Adjustment steps of clamping force:

1.    Loosen the fastening screw on the side of the lock nut. Rotate the locking nut at the back of the spindle counterclockwise so that the spindle is not clamped.
2.    Load the cone sleeve.
3.    Load the spring.
4.    Load the spring chuck.
5.    Install the spindle nut and lock it.
6.    Load the bar into the spindle and protrude the end face of the spring chuck.
7.    Clamp the spring chuck.
8.    Rotate the locking nut at the back of  the spindle clockwise until the spring chuck is just enough to clamp the bar.
9.    Loosen the spring chuck
10.    Continue to rotate the locking nut at the back end of the spindle clockwise for about 1/4 turns.
11.    Clamp the spring chuck again, check whether the clamping force is appropriate. Fine-tune the locking nut at the back end of the spindle until the spring chuck gets the ap-propriate clamping force.
12.    
13.    Tighten the fastening screw on the side of the locking nut.
14.    Confirm the position of the claw on the siding sleeve when clamping, and adjust the relative position of the claw on the sliding sleeve by clamping the connecting rod on the cylinder. 
15.    Lock the connecting rod with the nut on the cylinder.

7.11    Installation and Clamping Force adjustment of guide bushing 

1)    Use special hexagonal wrench to confirm that the locking screw on the left edge of the  locking screw has been relaxed.
2)    Cleaning the guide bushing  mounting hole and guide bushing , and add lubricating oil.
3)    Put the guide bushing  into the guide bushing, and the slot on the guide bushing  needs to correspond to the phase of the pin in the guide bushing.
4)    Put the bar into the guide bushing and extend the end face of the guide bushing . Use special wrench to rotate the guide bushing  to locking nut, tighten the guide bushing  gradually
5)    When tightened the guide bushing , the bar shall not be bitten to death, the bar can be pulled by hand flexibly (otherwise the  will be damaged). At the same time, there is no obvious gap between the bar and the guide bushing  (otherwise the cutting ef-fect of the part will be poor).
6)    After adjusting the clamping force of the guide bushing properly, use special hexa-gon wrench to locking the screw. And check the clamping force of the guide bushing  again. If the clamping force is not suitable, please repeat the above steps.

7.12    Application of non-guide bush 

The non-guide bush sleeve can be selected for parts with length-diameter ratio less than 3 .

Maximum length of machined parts without guide bush mode: 50mm .

The mode of the guide sleeve needs to be set correctly before using the machine tool. Different guide sleeve modes will call different Z1 axis soft limit. 

NC initial page facing right, find the “FUNCTION” feature option.   Find the “GUIDE BUSH” set-ting option in the “FUNCTION” feature option sub-page. Move the cursor to select the correct guide bush mode.

Guide bush mode setting page

Fig.36   Schematic diagram of synchronous guide bushing drive

Fig.37   Schematic diagram of unguided bushing structure

Steps of replace synchronous guide bush mode to non-guide bush mode:

1. Remove the spindle nut at the front of the spindle.
2. Remove the protective sheet metal of the spline belt.
3. Remove the protective cover at the front of the spindle.
4. Take off the spindle belt.
5. Take off the linkage bracket.
6. Fix the linkage bracket to the post.
7. Remove the protective sheet metal on the side of the synchronous guide sleeve.
8. Take off the synchronous guide sleeve drive belt.
9. Remove the synchronous guide sleeve.
10. Install the lagging for non-guide bush mode.
11. Install the spindle protective cover for non-guide bush mode.
12. Replace the protective sheet metal on the side of the guide bush seat.
13. Replace the spindle nut.
14. Set the guide bush mode to “non-guide bush” mode in the NC system.

1.     Set the thrust of the machine reasonably according to the material, diameter and weight of the bar.
