7.15    Adjustment of the bar pusher device

The bar pusher device is a device that discharging the workpiece from the back spindle. In order to deal with the situation that the workpiece cannot be discharged smoothly after executing the workpiece discharge command, installed with the stop alarm safety device. When replacing the ejector head, need to select the header that matching the workpiece diameter. If the randomly configured ejector head cannot meet the requirements of the workpiece, the user needs to make the ejector head by himself.

7.15.1 Diagram of bar pusher device

1.air blow connector

2. adjustment screw

3.supporting sleeve

4.locking screw

5. bar pusher tube

6. supporting tube

7.bar pusher head

8. connection plate

7.15.2    Installation and adjustment of bar pusher

1)    put（6）supporting tube into（3）supporting sleeve.
2)    locking（4）locking screw.
3)    install（3） supporting sleeve and（6）supporting tube on the spindle bracket, locking fixed screw for support sleeve.
4)    adjust (2) adjustment screw, concentricity of the (6) supporting tube in relation to the spin-dle bore.
5)    install（5）bar pusher tube to（8）connecting plate.
6)    install air blow connection on（5）bar pusher tube.
7)    install（7）bar pusher head on（5） bar pusher tube.
8)    put (5) bar pusher tube assembly into (6) supporting tube, and pull (5) bar pusher tube back and forth, to ensure that (5) bar pusher tube slides smoothly.
9)    install the (8) connecting plate on the bar pusher cylinder.
10)    control the bar pusher cylinder, drive （5 ）bar pusher tube to complete the bar pusher action. When (5) bar pusher tube is pushed, adjust the relative position of (5) bar pusher tube with respect to (8) the connecting plate to ensure that the bar pusher head can extend 1~2mm from the end face of the back spindle collect. Tighten the locking screw on the (8) connecting plate to lock the bar pusher tube.
11)    control the bar pusher cylinder to repeatedly extend and retract to ensure that the cylinder moves smoothly without jamming. Locking the (8) connecting plate with the bar pusher cylinder.

7.17 Work piece machining operations

Attention for work piece machining operations: 

1)    When there is an error in the programming of the parts processing program, the program must be modified correctly before continuing to running the program.
2)    After the programming is completed, the dry run of the program must be carried out with-out a tool installed in the machine. After the dry run is correct, the subsequent work can be continued.
3)    Install the tool, install the part blank, positioning each tool to pre-work position based on programming data, confirm whether there is the possibility of interference with the tool
4)    Press the "single block" button on the operation panel to lower the value of the feed rate override knob to start the automatic operation of a single block, and the next block can on-ly be run after the previous block runs without error. In single-stage operation, pay close at-tention to the errors caused by various command errors and tool offset setting errors. When you find that there is a possibility of wrong action, you should immediately press the emergency stop button to exit the operation. It can run automatically after all errors are eliminated.

Predict machine failure or early discovering, and controlling their losses to the production to a minimum, it is necessary to check the machine tools regularly regardless of whether there are failures. This chapter summarizes and explains the necessary inspection items starting from the daily inspection to once every 6 months.

8.1    Daily maintenance

The daily inspection is mainly a simple inspection that should be carried out before and during the operation of the machine tool every day. Please check the main points as shown below. In addition, the situations that need attention are not limited to those listed in this chapter, such as abnormal conditions of the machine tool, and abnormal sounds that people can feel during operation, temperature, damage, In case of abnormal odors, inspections must be carried out at any time. For this reason, the state of the machine tool during normal operation should be grasped at ordinary times. This is the most effective daily inspection.

8.2    Common failures and solutions

This chapter will introduce the handing methods of common faults. The treatment method of fault belongs to the common range, faults other than the setting may occur in the actual opera-tion. When the cause of the failure can not be identified or can not be handled, please contact Machinery customer service center for maintenance.

8.2.1    Related alarm of spindle and sub spindle chuck  

When the spindle chuck can not be opened, there will be a phenomenon that the machined parts are short or the processed bars can not be discharged.
•    Step to deal with:
1.    Check whether the gas source is normal.
2.    Check whether the clamping solenoid valve is working properly.
3.    Check whether the clamping cylinder is working properly.
4.    Check whether clamping induction switch is normal, loose or damaged.
5.    Check whether the clamping cylinder connecting rod is loose.
6.    Check whether the front and rear stroke of the claw is normal.
7.    Disassemble the spindle nut, clean the chuck and taper sleeve, clean the inner hole of the spindle。
8.    Check whether the taper sleeve has any strain, check whether the taper sleeve and the inner hole of the spindle are killed.
9.    Replace a new chuck.

After confirming the above items, the problem has not been solved, please contact Machinery customer service canter for maintenance.

8.2.2    Low cooling flow rate 

When the machine tool uses cutting oil, there is a risk of fire when the work piece is not cooled enough. When the machine tool is in short supply of cooling oil, it should be dealt with in time.

•    Step to deal with：
1.    Check whether the cooling line is loose and fall off.
2.    Check whether the cooling pump is working properly.
3.    Check whether the cooler has enough oil.
4.    Clean the chip contact cavity.
5.    Clean the filter of the cooler.

8.2.3    Overheating alarm of spindle and sub spindle 

The spindle and sub spindle of this machine tool adopt the structure of built-in motorized spin-dle. A temperature sensor is preset in the spindle. Alarm will be given when the spindle is oper-ating abnormally and the temperature is too high.

•    Step to deal with：
1.    Check the I/O module of the machine tool. 
The input point ‘I16’ is the overheating detection of spindle;
The input point ‘I23’ is the overheating detection of sub spindle,
Damage to the input module will trigger this kind of alarm.
If the I/O module of the machine tool is damaged, please contact Machinery customer service center for repair.
2.    Check whether the spindle oil cooler is working properly.
3.    If the spindle is really too hoot and whether there is a scorched smell. The spindle coil may have been damaged. Please contact Machinery customer service center for repair.
4.    If the speed of the spindle is abnormal. Maybe the spindle coil or the encoder is damaged.  Please contact Machinery customer service center for repair.
5.    If the spindle load fluctuates abnormally under no load. The spindle coil may have been damaged. Please contact Machinery customer service center for repair.
6.    If the three resistance of the spindle coil is unbalanced. Maybe the spindle coil is damaged. Please contact Machinery customer service center for repair.
7.    If the earth resistance of the spindle coil is not infinite. Maybe the spindle coil is damaged. Please contact Machinery customer service center for repair.
8.     Check if the spindle power cable is damaged or loose.
9.    Inspect the spindle encoder cable for damage or loose.
10.    Inspect the spindle drive for damage. 

8.2.4    Abnormal alarm of ejector

The material ejector device is arranged in the sub spindle. For the parts whose length is less than 80 mm, the material can be ejected from the front end of the sub spindle through the ma-terial ejector device.
If the parts in the sub spindle are not ejected in time, in the subsequent processing, the parts in the sub spindle will interfere with other parts of the machine tool or subsequent parts.
For the structure and adjustment of the jacking device, please refer to: 6.16 Adjustment of the bar pusher device.

When performing the jacking action, if the machine tool does not receive the signal of the jack-ing sensor switch, it will issue a corresponding alarm.
•    Step to deal with:
1.    Check whether the gas source is normal.
2.    Check whether the solenoid valve is working properly.
3.    Check the trachea for damage.
4.    Check whether the cylinder throttle valve is adjusted properly.
5.    Check whether the cylinder action is stuttered.
6.    Inspect the cylinder induction switch for loosening or damage.
7.    Check whether the ejector rod is bent and whether is works smoothly.
8.    Check whether there are any residual work pieces or material heads in the sub spindle and clean them up in time.

8.2.5    Alarm exceeding torque limit

In the machining process of the machine tool, when the drilling tool is broken, the tool wear is serious, and the cutting tool is damaged, the machining torque will increase instantly in the fol-low-up machining. If it is not dealt with in time, it will be dangerous to install the machine. By reasonably controlling the torque output of each shaft, the occurrence of similar situation can be avoided to a certain extent.

Please adjust the torque output of each shaft according to the actual working condition.

How to deal with the occurrence of relevant alarm：

8.2.6    Lost position command alarm

•     Alarm description
The controller checks at any time whether the error between the feedback instruction and the output instruction is within the predetermined error range after the controller stops outputting a certain axial instruction for 1 second. If it is not within this range, the alarm is triggered.

•     Possible reasons
1.    The phenomenon of hindrance in the movement of the mechanism.
2.    Unexpected servo enable occurs in the servo driver.
3.    The possible reasons for the loss of the data sent by the CPU board to the shaft card are: abnormal CPU board, abnormal shaft card and poor contact with the shaft card.
4.    Poor contact or disconnection of the command transmission line from the controller to the servo drive.
5.    Local interference.

•      Method of exclusion
1.    Check whether the lubrication system of the machine tool.
2.    Check to see if the screw is stuck.
3.    Check whether the servo motor power and encoder cable are connected properly.
4.    Check whether the servo motor power and encoder cable are damaged.
5.    Check whether the driver is powered on properly and if there is any missing phase.
6.    Do not turn off the machine tool after the alarm occurs, check whether the date of diagnostic function Nos. 8, 9 and 10 converge to 0.
If the values of diagnostic variables 8, 9 and 10 remain unchanged, please re-find the origin. After finding the origin, observe the diagnostic variables 24, 25, 26 and 40, 41, 42:
If any of the values of variables 24, 25 and 26 is not 0, there is something wrong with the feedback loop.
If any of the values of variables 40, 41 and 42 is not 0, it means that there is a loss of instructions in the circuit of the controller motor.
If the variables 24, 25, 26 and 40, 41, 42 are not 0, the possibility of signal interfer-ence is greater, which is shown by the gradual increase of the values of variables 8, 9 and 10 in processing. The specific point of failure may be the poor contact between the CPU board and the shaft card. Try to replace the CPU board and the shaft card.
7.    Try to replace the motor and drive.

8.2.7    Tracking error alarm  

•     Alarm description
Due to the servo characteristics, the positioning of the servo motor can not immediately re-spond to the instructions of the controller, but will lag behind. When the backwardness is not within the allowable range, the controller will issue an alarm that the tracking error exceeds.

•     Possible reasons
1.    The mechanism does not move smoothly.
2.    Poor contact of wire rod.
3.    The deceleration time set by the controller parameters is too short.
4.    Drive or motor failure.

•      Method of exclusion
1.    Check whether the machine tool is installed.
2.    Check the lubrication circuit to ensure that the moving parts of the machine tool are fully lubricated
3.    Check the wiring of the machine tool to ensure that the motor, driver and other lines and connected properly and are not damaged.
4.    Adjust the acceleration and deceleration time of the corresponding shaft.
5.    The airborne machine tool reconfirms that the machine mechanism moves smoothly and there is no interference.
6.    If you still can’t rule out the alarm. Please contact Machinery customer service center for repair

1.    Diagnostic variable 32：X-axis reasonable tracking error
2.    Diagnostic variable 33：Y-axis reasonable tracking error
3.    Diagnostic variable 34：Z-axis reasonable tracking error

8.2.8    Absolute position battery is low power

Absolute position encoders are used in this machine tool servo motor. There is no need to re-turn to zero after the machine is shut down and restarted. When the machine tool is shut down, the encoder of the servo motor will record the position information of each linear axis. When the machine tool is shut down, the power supply of the servo motor encoder is provided by the bat-tery behind the machine tool. when the battery is low, the alarm of  “absolute position battery is low” will be issued.

Precautions when replacing batteries:
1.    Battery specification：LR20 Model（N0.1battery）
2.    The battery should be replaced when the machine tool is powered on normally, other-wise the absolute position information or the machine tool will be lost.

 

8.2.9    Absolute position zero lost

Absolute position encoders are used in this machine tool servo motor. There is no need to re-turn to zero after the machine is shut down and restarted. When the machine tool is shut down, the encoder of the servo motor will record the position information of each linear axis. When the machine tool is turned on, the system does not read the position information of the servo motor, their will be an alarm of “absolute position origin is set”.

•     Possible reasons
1.    The machine tool is shut down for a long time, and the battery is consumed in the absolute position.
2.    Absolute position when the battery is low, the battery is not replaced in time.
3.    Damage to servo motor or drive.
4.    A new motor or drive has been replaced.

•     Method of exclusion
1.    Power on the machine tool at least once a week.
2.    Change the battery in time when the battery is low power.
3.    Re Set the absolute zero point.
Machine tool zero, please refer to 5.6 machine tool itinerary.

•     Setting steps of zero position
1.    Switch the machine tool to hand wheel control mode.
2.    Control the movement of each straight axis to the origin position through the hand wheel. refer to 5.6 machine tool itinerary.
3.    Switch the machine tool to origin mode.
4.    Use the NC function key to enter the NC origin setting page, refer to the following steps:
“parameter setting” → “tandem parameter” → “general tandem axis” → “absolute posi-tion origin”.
5.    Move the cursor, select the corresponding axis, and complete the zero setting.

8.3    The attention of maintenance

1.    Before repairing the machine tool, professional maintenance personnel should be famil-iar with the electrical control principle of the machine tool and the operation steps of the machine tool.
2.    During the use of the machine tool, the dust in the electrical installation niche must be cleaned regularly, and the power supply must be cut off when cleaning the electrical in-stallation niche.
3.    When the machine tool is shut down for a long time, the machine tool needs to be ener-gized once a week to run the machine tool at a low speed. The running time is generally not less than 2 hours.
4.    When the machine tool fails, first check whether the power supply and each control voltage are normal; whether the circuit breakers and switches are in normal state; whether the connectors and plugs are in good contact and whether there is any loose-ness.
5.    It is strictly forbidden to plug and unplug any connecting devices when the machine is powered on.
6.    It is strictly forbidden to solder electronic circuits when the soldering iron is energized.
7.    The positions of switches and stoppers on the machine tool have been adjusted before leaving the factory. Users should not adjust them easily, so as not to affect the normal use of the machine tool.

If the user cannot solve the fault that appears, please contact with us in time for proper han-dling of the fault.

1.1  Protection Warnings

Warning: This machine tool is equipped with relevant warning labels in areas that may pose a risk of personal injury. These labels serve as warnings to the operator. Therefore, under no circumstances should the warning labels be removed. Operators must pay attention to the content of the warnings to ensure personal protection and avoid injury.

Figure1 Warning Label Placement 1

Figure2 Warning Label Placement 2

 1）Machine Operation Warning Sign  2）Maintenance & Service Precautions 

 3）Injury Hazard Sign 1         4）Injury Hazard Sign 2 



 5）Electric Shock Warning Sign     6）Grounding Safety Symbol 

 7）Lubrication Point Indicator       8）Filter Cleaning Reminder 

1.2 Safety Device Warnings

Warning: Safety devices do not guarantee absolute safety in the event of accidents or hazardous conditions. When using this machine tool, always adhere to the relevant precautions.

1.2.1 Installation Location of Safety Devices

Installation Location of Safety Devices as shown in Figure 3.

Figure 3 Installation Location of Safety Devices

1.2.2 Functional Description of Safety Devices

  1. Main Circuit BreakerIf excessive current is detected in the machine, thepower supply will be automatically cut off.
  2. Emergency Stop Button

A red emergency stop button is located on the machine's control panel. In emergencies such as fire, earthquake, or lightning, or when evacuation is necessary, press this button to stop the machine before leaving.

Note: Pressing the emergency stop button during machine operation may damage the workpiece, cutting tools, and the machine itself.

To reset the emergency, stop state, ensure safety and then rotate the emergency stop button clockwise.

1.2.3 Precautions Before Operation

  1. Before starting the machine, the operator must understand the following:
    • Location of the emergency stop button and safety devices
    • Content of all warning labels
    • Method to stop the machine in an emergency
    • Functions, operation methods, and sequence of switches and buttons on the control panel
    • Correct startup and shutdown procedures
    • Handling methods for abnormal situations
    • Method to cut off the main circuit breaker
  2. Operators must not operate the machine under conditions that may compromise personal safety or the safe operation of the machine, such as after consuming alcohol, excessive fatigue, or poor health.
  3. Operators must wear appropriate work attire (avoid loose or oversized clothing), a cap, and safety shoes. Remove gloves, rings, accessories, ties, or other items that may get caught in the machine.
  4. To prevent slipping, tripping, or falling, ensure no hazardous objects are placed around the machine.
  5. Do not place flammable objects near the machine.
  6. Do not operate the machine if the safety devices are not functioning properly.
  7. Ensure the emergency stop button functions correctly.
  8. Do not modify the machine or control circuits without authorization, as this may cause personal injury or machine damage.
  9. Use lubricating, non-corrosive, and non-flammable cutting oils.
  10. Before operating the machine, ensure that tools, collet, etc., are installed correctly.
  11. Before operating the machine, confirm that the coolant level is sufficient and that necessary areas are adequately cooled. Regularly remove swarf from the cutting area and Chip Conveyor.
  12. Do not operate the machine with the protective guard removed!
  13. Do not process flammable, explosive, or dust-generating materials! If such materials need to be processed, additional protective devices must be installed. Consult our technical staff for further information.

1.2.4 Precautions During Operation

  1. Do not open the electrical cabinet door while the main circuit breaker is in the "ON" position.
  2. During machine operation, do not touch or approach cutting tools, rotating parts, or moving parts of the machine.
  3. When operating the machine, visually confirm the operation switches before pressing them.

1.2.5 Precautions for Shutting Down the Machine

Shutdown sequence:

Reset/Stop the machine --- Press the emergency stop button on the control panel

Turn off the system power, confirm that the display is off and the control panel indicators are off --- Cut off the main circuit breaker 

1.2.6 Emergency Handling Measures

1.2.6.1 In case of fire, earthquake, or lightning:

If there is sufficient time:

Stop the machine immediately, press the NC system power switch, confirm that the LCD display is off and the panel lights are off, then cut off the main circuit breaker.

If there is no time:

Press the emergency stop button immediately, then cut off the main circuit breaker. 2) In case of sudden power failure:

After a sudden power failure, turn off the main circuit breaker and press the emergency stop button.

1.2.6.2 Resuming operation after an emergency:

After stopping the machine due to an emergency or sudden power failure, restart the machine in the following sequence:

Check for tool damage and replace if necessary.

Remove any unfinished workpieces from the machine.---Restart the machine。

1.2.7 Precautions for Machine Storage and Transportation

The machine is packaged with waterproof materials, and rust preventive oil is applied to key components. The packaging box is designed with anti-vibration and anti-impact measures to ensure safe transportation and storage within a temperature range of -25°C to 55°C. However, the packaging box must not be inverted or tilted beyond 15°, and it should not be subjected to severe impacts or vibrations to avoid damaging internal components.

When the machine tool is not in operation for an extended period, it is necessary to perform protective operations by applying anti-rush oil to the machine tool and removing the power supply cables;cover the machine with a plastic film sleeve for dust prevention; block the power cable entry holes in the electrical niche of the machine base to prevent rodent damage.

4.1 Unpacking the Machine

After opening the outer packaging, first remove the standard accessories, coolant tank, and other accessories from the bottom plate of the packaging box. Then, remove the four fastening nuts connecting the machine to the bottom plate.

4.1 Hoisting and Positioning the Machine

  1. Use two steel rods with a diameter of Φ65 mm (made of 20# high-quality low-carbon steel, each 2.5 m in length, with a safety factor greater than 5). Insert them into the two sets of Φ70 holes in the base. As shown in Figure 4, hook the lifting slings onto the steel rods (the lifting slings must have a safety factor greater than 5, and they should not come into contact with any parts of the machine tool. If necessary, use wooden blocks to securely keep the slings away from the machine tool components, or use a dedicated lifting frame).

    Figure 4 Lifting Diagram
  2. Gradually tension the lifting slings while checking the center of gravity of the machine tool to ensure safety.
  3. Once the machine tool has been moved to its installation location and is still suspended in the air, take the six provided leveling screws and lock nuts and screw them together. Then, from the foot installation cavity, thread the leveling screws into the six foot bolt holes on the base, ensuring the spherical ends protrude from the bottom surface of the base.

    Figure 5 Foundation Pad Placement Diagram

  4. Place the six provided foundation pads in the correct positions at the machine tool’s installation site as shown in the diagram.

    Figure 6 Lowering and Alignment Diagram

  5. Gradually lower the machine tool and adjust its position so that the six leveling screws align with the recesses on the foundation pads.
  6. Gently place the machine tool on the six foundation pads and adjust the leveling screws so that the distance between the bottom surface of the machine tool’s base foot screws and the ground is 35mm.

Warning: The following precautions must be followed during the lifting of the machine tool. Failure to do so may result in the machine tool falling (damage) and causing serious personal injury.

  1. Lifting personnel must hold nationally recognized qualification certificates.
  2. Rolling the machine tool is strictly prohibited 
  3. Anti-slip pads (rubber or cloth) must be added to the parts where the lifting rods and lifting cables come into contact.
  4. Ensure the lifting slings can withstand the machine tool’s weight (approx. 3750kg) with adequate safety factor.
  5. The machine tool should not tilt during lifting, and impacts and vibrations should be avoided as much as possible.

4.2.1 Forklift Transportation

Figure 7 Machine Tool Forklift Diagram

Warning: The following precautions must be followed during the lifting of the machine tool. Failure to do so may result in the machine tool falling (damage) and causing serious personal injury.

  1. Forklift operators must hold nationally recognized qualification certificates.
  2. Ensure the forklift has sufficient load capacity for the machine tool’s weight (approx. 3750kg) and complies with required safety margins.
  3. The machine tool should not tilt severely during forklift transportation, and impacts and vibrations should be avoided as much as possible.

4.3 Machine Tool Installation

4.3.1 Foundation Requirements

  1. The foundation for installing the machine tool must have a load-bearing capacity of at least 3750 kg/m², and the thickness of the foundation should not be less than 150 mm.
  2. The foundation must extend outward by more than 300 mm from the perimeter of the machine tool.
  3. The machine tool should be installed on a solid, level ground and should not be affected by vibrations from other machines.
  4. When the machine tool is installed on a floor, the structural strength and vibration frequency of the floor must be thoroughly evaluated and approved by the building design unit or an authoritative department.
  5. The placement of the machine tool should allow sufficient space for operation and maintenance as indicated in the drawings.

4.3.2 Environmental Conditions for Machine Tool Operation

  1. Ambient temperature: 0°C to 45°C during operation; -20°C to 55°C during non-operation; temperature change ≤ 20°C per hour.
  2. Relative humidity: Typically 10% to 75% (no condensation), short-term (within one month) 5% to 95% (no condensation).
  3. During operation ≤ 0.5G, during non-operation ≤ 1G.
  4. Avoid areas where chips, water, or oil may splash.
  5. Keep away from welding and other electrical machining equipment.
  6. Avoid areas with strong electromagnetic interference.
  7. Avoid direct sunlight exposure.

4.3.3 Coolant System Connection

  1. Push the coolant tank into the lower part of the machine tool from the right side, aligning it with the machine tool's external sheet metal.
  2. Place the chip box into the coolant tank and align it with the machine tool's drainage port.
  3. Cover the chip collection box.
  4. Connect the coolant pump to the machine tool using a hose and secure the connection with a hose clamp.
  5. Plug in the coolant pump power supply.
  6. Plug in the coolant tank liquid level detection switch.
  7. Start the coolant pump briefly to test the rotation direction. If the rotation direction is opposite to the direction marked on the pump, change the power phase sequence to adjust the rotation direction.
  8. Fill the coolant tank (approximately 200L capacity).
    (Please select the appropriate coolant based on the material being processed.)
  9. Coolant connection is complete.

    Figure 8 Diagram of Machine Tool Coolant System

4.3.4 Machine Tool Lubrication System

The lubrication pump oil tank has a capacity of 2L, with an effective capacity of 1.5L. The recommended lubricant is ISO VG-68. Initially, add 1.5L of lubricant to the lubrication pump. After the machine tool has been running for a period, replenish the lubricant in a timely manner.

Note: The lubricant must be clean; otherwise, it may damage the machine tool's guideways and ball screws.

4.3.5 Machine Tool Air Supply Requirements

Air supply connection hose for the machine tool: Φ10mm.

Compressed Air Requirements:Pressure 0.5MPa、Flow Rate 80Nl/min.

Ensure the air supply is dry and clean. Regularly drain any residual moisture from the air compressor, and add a secondary filtration device if necessary.

4.3.6 Machine Tool Power Supply Requirements

  1. Machine tool power supply voltage: 3-phase 380V±10%, 50Hz±2%
  2. Machine tool power: 12kVA (including high voltage: 15kVA).

Note: The machine tool grounding must be secure and reliable. The neutral line of the power supply must not be used as a protective grounding line.

Figure 9 Diagram of Machine Tool External Interfaces

4.3.7 Spindle Cooling Oil

Recommended spindle cooling oil: Great Wall No. 7 spindle oil or Mobil No. 4 spindle oil.

The machine tool spindle uses an internal circulation cooling system. An appropriate amount of spindle oil is added at the factory. During transportation, some spindle oil may be lost. Ensure the spindle cooling oil is sufficient when using the machine tool for the first time.

During normal operation, there is no loss of spindle cooling oil. If abnormal loss of spindle cooling oil is observed, check for leaks in the cooling oil circuit and contact the manufacturer for repair if necessary.

The machine tool is equipped with a professional oil chiller for circulating cooling of the spindle. Refer to the oil chiller's user manual for usage and maintenance instructions.

Regularly clean the external filter screen of the oil chiller. A clogged filter screen will restrict airflow, reducing the cooling efficiency of the oil chiller and causing it to overload. Prolonged overload operation can damage the oil chiller.

Figure 10 Diagram of Oil Chiller Cleaning

4.3.8 Machine Tool Leveling

  1. Move the spindle head to the negative limit position of the Z1 axis.
  2. Place the leveling plate horizontally above the two guide rails of the Z1 axis.
  3. Place a precision level on the leveling plate.
  4. Adjust the machine tool's leveling screws until the level reads within 0.04mm/m (both axial and longitudinal adjustments are required).
  5. Tighten the lock nuts on the leveling screws.

    Figure 11 Machine Horizontal Leveling Schematic

The cutting reference data described in this chapter are standard cutting conditions. In practical applications, adjustments should be made flexibly based on the workpiece material, tool geometry, tool material and coating, and cooling conditions.

1.1  Key features

The SYIL R1 is a turnkey product designed to automate a SYIL X series. The design is compact, portable, quick to install and only requires basic engineering skills. The unit is electrically and pneumatically operated.

  • Ideal for low volume and high part mix manufacturing
  • Standard pre-wired and assembled equipment
  • Smooth, repeatable and automatic loading and unloading

1.3  Intended use

The unit is only intended for the following uses

  • The unit may only be used within the scope of its technical specifications
  • Appropriate use of the unit includes compliance with all instructions in this document
  • The unit is intended for installation in a SYIL X The applicable guidelines for the CNC machine must be complied with.
  • It is intended to only be used with a single machine at a time
  • The standard package is intended to be equipped with a single gripper pneumatic tooling
  • The operator should only load and unload using the intended access door
  • All installation require a safety risk assessment review
  • The product can only produce a single workpiece per program
  • The product is intended to be use with the standard grid provided with the unit

 The SYIL R1 is a pre-package solution intended to automate a SYIL X series CNC machine. This manual describes how to operate, maintain, and the requirements to integrate the solution to an existing system.

3.1 READ INSTRUCTIONS THOROUGHLY BEFORE INSTALLING

This section is intended to guide the integrator to facilitate and expedite the installation. Only qualified personnel are authorized to perform the installation of the unit.

3.2  Transportation and unpacking

The unit is shipped in a standardized pallet which can easily be moved to the desired location using a forklift. Upon receiving, inspect the unit for any visible damage.

The unit is shipped with the robot and most of the hardware already installed on the base to minimize the startup time.

Shipping specifications

  • Dimensions [mm]: 1042(W) x 1475(L) x 1525(H)
  • Weight [kg]: 375

3.2.1  Scope of delivery

All the hardware needed to install the unit are provided in the form of kits. These kits are located in the hardware box along with the feet of the unit.

3.2.2    Handling the unit


The lifting hook should not be used to lift several modules at the same time. Use appropriate technique according to the type of hook mounted on the unit.

When applicable, information about lifting point, weight of the unit and center of gravity will be provided in the shipping document.

Use designated lifting point when unpacking the unit from the shipping crate. Install provided foot to the base.

The unit must be positioned in such a way that the robot can access the CNC work holding fixture.

3.5  Basic integration

Here are the basic steps the integrator should follow to get started with the unit

  • Familiarize themselves with the documentation
  • Ensure all the safety protocols listed in the present documentation are followed
  • Perform a hazard and risk assessment following the installation of the final application
  • Ensure the integration with the external machine is complete
  • Calibrate all the needed work objects
  • Using a calibration workpiece, perform a basic teach-in
  • Perform the program with the robot at low speed
  • Refine as needed

The following section describes the interval that the preventive maintenance should be performed. The goal of these maintenances is to detect irregularities to prevent potential issues. Any damaged parts should be replaced before they cause any problems to the unit. The maintenance of the robot must be followed. The details are in the robot manual.

5.1  Maintenance schedule

Maintenance intervals

Interval

Maintenance to be performed

Once per week

The unit should be cleaned

Check if the finger adjustments are still valid

Once per month

Check if the safety functions are still compliant

Check if the gripper(s) are still functioning normally Check for any leakage in the pneumatic hose

Once per 3 months

Check the cable for any signs of damage

Check for discontinuity in the connections Tighten the threaded cable connections

When necessary

Clean or replace faulty components

Adjust the air pressure

5.2  Mechanical maintenance

Cleaning the unit

Interval:          Once per week

Description: While the robot is in its home position, clean all surfaces from any debris that could cause problems. When cleaning the polycarbonate panel, remove any dust using a soft, clean brush. When no dust is left, use a moist towel with anti-static plastic cleaner to wipe it clean.

 

Checking the finger adjustments

Interval:        Once per week

Description: While the robot is in its home position, verify that all gripper fingers are still properly adjusted.

 

Verifying the gripper system Interval:   Once per month

Description: While the robot is in its home position, visually inspect the gripper system for signs of mechanical wears. Any damaged part shall be replaced.

 

Lubricating the gripper system

Description: For more information on the gripper system lubrication, refer to the Schunk manuals.

 

Robot maintenance

Description: For any maintenance pertaining to the robot, refer to the product manual of the robot.

5.3  Electrical maintenance

Checking the safety functions compliance

Interval:        Once per month

Description: Verify the all emergency stop and safety switches are operating properly.

 

Checking the cable for damage

Interval:                   Once per 3 months

Description: Inspect the cable for damage and remove any source of potential damage like sharp edges.

 

Checking the electrical connections

Interval:   Once per 3 months

Description: Verify the continuity of all the connections and ensure that they are properly secured in a way that leaves no risk of play. Tighten any loose connections.

 

Controller maintenance
Description: For any maintenance pertaining to the robot’s controller, refer to the product manual of the controller.

SYIL R1 Development Guide Document

This document explains the contents of the SYIL R1 Development Guide in terms of safety interface, electrical interface, pneumatic interface, Signals interface, SYIL user interface functions and robot sequence.

Safety Interface

The safety of the robot needs to be linked with the safety of the CNC. On this product, we will use two different safety features: Access Doors and Emergency Stop

We are planning to use two different modes with the robot: T1 and EXT.

T1: Teach mode à It’s possible to jog and test the robot program at slow speed with the cell door opened.

EXT: External mode à It’s possible to control the robot and run program from the Siemens controller at full speed if the safety is acknowledged.

Access Doors

The CNC is normally equipped with a dual channel safety switch on the main operator door. For the actual project, we will add two new doors. It’s important to safely control the side loading door (2).

Here are the 3 access doors:

  1. CNC main front door for the operator access
  2. Side Auto-Door for the robot access.
  3. Robot cell access to change and manipulate the parts

 

Emergency Stop

The CNC is normally equipped with dual channel emergency stop on the controller. For the actual project, the robot will need to know if the SYIL E-Stop is pressed.

Wiring

We will need to exchange some signal between the R1 and the CNC. The R1 needs to know when it’s ok to let the robot move. By example, if the operator door is open, Safety Door Ch. A-B circuits must be opened or if the Side loading door is open, CNC must not start machining.

You will see below an example of wiring.

This section describes the safe practices of all the Waybo products. Some safe practices in this section may not apply to the equipment in this manual.

FAILURE TO COMPLY TO THE INFORMATION PRESENT IN THIS MANUAL MAY RESULT TO PHYSICAL INJURIES OR CAUSE DAMAGE TO MATERIAL PROPERTY

2.1  Writing Conventions

Failure to comply with these requirements will result in serious injury

Failure to comply with these requirements could result in serious injury

Failure to comply with these recommendations may result in equipment damage

These tips provide additional information relevant to first time users

2.2  Safety obligations for the integrator of the complete system

This manual only highlights basic safety practices. The integrator is responsible for understanding and complying to local laws and regulations regarding safety.

The integrator is responsible for the training of their company personnel regarding the safe usage of the equipment. This manual shall be made available to be read and understood by all personnel using the equipment.

The integrator shall comply to all safety measures issued by the manufacturer of all components used in the equipment.

The integrator is responsible for the installation and for the proper use of the equipment during normal operation.

In the event of a relocation, the integrator is responsible for providing all the required information necessary for safe use of the equipment.

The integrator is responsible for ensuring that the safety measures of the complete system are designed and implemented as to protect people working with the unit.

Entrance access shall be determined prior to the installation. Always use these entrances when going in the equipment perimeter. The complete system shall be designed in a way that allows safe access to any personnel needing to perform operation, adjustment, and/or maintenance.

After the installation, the following must be done by the integrator

  • Perform a hazard and risk assessment following the installation of the complete system
  • Ensure the integration with the external machine is complete

The integrator is responsible for the safety of the complete system and its final application.

2.3  Safety during initial installation

Only qualified personnel are authorized to perform the installation of the unit.

Ensure that there is sufficient stability around the CNC Machine for a safe installation

The user is liable to any damage caused while using additional equipment not supplied by the manufacturer

2.4  Safety while handling the unit

Before lifting any equipment, a maintenance and verification of the certified lifting equipment should be done by qualified personnel.

Always use proper technique when lifting the unit (i.e. Never lift at an angle if the hooks are designed for vertical loads only).

Eliminate or reduce any possible hazards, including slipping, tripping, falling, etc. prior to handling the unit

Always take every precaution reasonable to protect workers while manually handling objects.

Never walk or stand near a suspended heavy load. The potential fall of the load could result in serious injury. In the event where it cannot be avoided, restrain the load by using the appropriate tools.

Always ensure that the eyebolts used to handle the equipment have a minimum thread engagement between the eyebolt and its tapped hold of 1-1/2 times the diameter of its thread. Nuts on through-eyebolt should always be self-locking. The shoulder should always seat uniformly against the surface on which they bear.

Always make sure that the load is applied in the plane of the eye. If the hook does not fit into the eyebolt, use a shackle to prevent tip loading.

2.5  Personal protective equipment

When using the unit, the operator should show caution and good sense. Thereas, the following should always be abided by the operators

  • Avoid loose clothing to prevent potential entanglement in the machinery
  • Never wear any jewellery near the machine
  • Long hair should be securely confined

Personal protective equipment must be worn when working around the equipment. Therefore, safety glasses, safety helmet and safety boots shall be worn in accordance with the risk-assessment made prior to using the equipment.

When working in an environment where noises exceed permissible limits, the use of hearing protection must be worn.

Gloves shall be worn when the risk of cut, or abrasion is present

Milling and cutting operations can produce a considerable amount of dust and particulate matter. The use of respiratory mask is advised.

2.6  Safety while operating the equipment

The unit may only be used in accordance with its intended use.

When the equipment is in use, any contact with moving parts shall not be possible.

Always check that the perimeter is clear before starting the unit. An operator shall always be present throughout the whole startup process.

Always check that the perimeter is clear and that nobody is still inside the perimeter when closing a safety door, resetting an emergency stop or restarting the unit.

It is strictly forbidden to use any tool to reach an elevated part of the unit while it is in operation.

Before pressurizing the pneumatic system, make sure that the system is in good working order.

Entrance access and electrical panels shall always remain unobstructed.

Electrical panel shall never be opened while powered ON due to potential arc flash and electrical shock.

The location and functionality of all controls shall be known by the operator prior to its use.

During normal operation of the unit, if the operator encounters a situation that appears to be dangerous, he shall shut down the unit and immediately seek advise from a supervisor. The operator shall never take a risk that could result in an injury.

Never touch or enter the path of a potential moving part. The failure to do so could result in serious injury or even death. A lockout procedure shall be performed ensuring the immobilization of the equipment before any intervention is made on the unit.

Ensure that nobody is within the restricted area during automatic operation

Always be aware of the pinch point hazard caused by the possible compressed air stored in the gripper system.

Only change gripper fingers if no residual energy can be released

Workpiece held in the gripper for an extended period of time in a depressurized state can fall causing a potential crushing hazard. When possible, make sure to remove the workpiece from the gripper prior to depressurizing the system.

2.7  Safety regarding the safety devices

Never remove protective guards or any other safety devices except where necessary for maintenance purposes.

In the event of a missing safety device, the operator shall shut down the unit and inform a supervisor. The unit shall remain inoperative until the device is replaced.

To prevent eventual damage to any safety mechanism, care shall be taken when using said mechanism.

The emergency stop should only be used in the event of a risk of injury or potential material damage requiring the immediate stop of the unit.

Before any start-up sequences, a standard procedure shall be done to check if any safety devices are not working properly. The operator should also check for potential breakage,

blockage, leak or any abnormality. In the event of any issue, the problem shall be resolved before starting the unit.

All safety and warning sign shall be kept clean and legible. In the event of a damaged sign, the appropriate step shall be taken to replace it.

2.8  Safety while performing maintenance of the unit

No modifications may be made to the unit without authorization. Unauthorized modifications will result in loss of warranty and liability claim.

Ensure the air pressure has been released before performing any maintenance to the equipment.

Always ensure that the unit cannot be started unintentionally when working in the restricted area.

It is strictly prohibited to remove any safety devices, except when performing maintenance. In such event, an alternative safety measure shall be determined by authorized personnel, and all safety devices shall be replaced after the maintenance is completed.

Any maintenance shall be carried out by trained and qualified personnel in accordance with the applicable local regulations.

The following shall be verified before performing any maintenance to the unit:

  • The E-Stop shall be activated
  • The voltage supply shall be shut down
  • The air supply shall be disconnected using the appropriate lockout device

2.9  Limitation of liability

Any information given in this manual regarding safety shall not be construed as a warranty by Waybo that the unit will not cause injury or damage even if all safety instructions are complied with.

The information does not cover how to design, install and operate a complete system, nor does it cover all peripheral equipment that can influence the safety of the entire system.

Liability cannot be accepted if injury or damaged has been caused for any of the following reasons:

  • Use of the unit in other ways than intended
  • Incorrect maintenance and/or maintenance carried out by non-qualified personnel
  • Operating the unit with safety device that are faulty, defective, not in their intended location and/or in any other way not working.
  • Making non-authorized modifications in the unit
  • Foreign objects

  2. Pick next part in the tray
  3. Go inside CNC
  4. Drop raw part in work holding
  5. Go outside CNC
  6. Home

Machining cycle

  1. Home
  2. Go inside CNC
  3. Pick finished part from work holding
  4. Go outside CNC
  5. Home
  6. Drop finished part on the tray

; ENDLOOP

Electrical Interface

The CNC supplies the electrical power for the automated solution. The R1 system requirement 230VAC - 16 amps.

 

Pneumatic Interface

The CNC supplies the pneumatic for the automated solution. The R1 system requires a 10mm hose at 90-120 PSI.

Signals Interface (I/O)

For the project, the Siemen controller will need to have some 24 VDC input and output available. We will also connect the KUKA controller with an Ethernet cable RJ45. Here are the signals list:

CNC

Vise (hard wiring):

Output#1: Open_Vise_1

Output#2: Close_Vise_1

 

Auto-Door (hard wiring):

Output#3: Open_AutoDoor

Output#4: Close_AutoDoor

Input#1: AutoDoor_Opened à Status must be shared with the robot

Input#2: AutoDoor_Closed à Status must be shared with the robot

 

Robot (fieldbus):

Outputs from SYIL to R1:

Gripper1:         

Open_Gripper_1

Close_Gripper_1

Gripper2 (option):      

Open_Gripper_2

Close_Gripper_2

Clean:

Clean_ON

Inputs from R1 to SYIL:

Robot position:            

Robot_Inside_CNC

Robot_Outside_CNC

Robot_Inside_R1

Robot_Outside_R1
